act_0000.subject: Character
act_0000.raw: If you choose this op- tion, create a non-powered version of your character that doesn’t have any of its Powers, has human-level Abilities, and may even have lower ranks of Skills and completely different Advantages.

act_0001.subject: Damage
act_0001.raw: A shotgun can fire shot, which does Damage 5 with Accurate 1 due to the spread, but Limited to Dam- age 3 against targets with Protection.

act_0002.subject: Power
act_0002.raw: Rifles designed for long-range use, typically in conjunction with a powerful scope or targeting system.

act_0003.subject: Character
act_0003.raw: A bow-wielding character may have various “trick” ar- rows with different powers, typically handled as devices.

act_0004.subject: Defense
act_0004.raw: SECRET ORIGINS *If you rolled Vampire for Abilities, then you have Abilities 44, Powers 73, and Defenses 17;

act_0005.subject: Defense
act_0005.raw: otherwise, you have Abili- ties 62, Powers 52, and Defenses 20.

act_0006.subject: Power
act_0006.raw: Some Supernatural Creatures attempt to go against the grain and use their powers for good.

act_0007.subject: Power
act_0007.raw: Some Supernatural Creatures only have their powers at night, or lose their powers when on holy ground or when they assume human form...

act_0008.subject: Attack
act_0008.raw: COMBAT, RANKED You have a +1 bonus to close attacks checks per rank in this advantage.

act_0009.subject: Ability
act_0009.raw: For capability with a particular type of attack, use the Close Combat skill.

act_0010.subject: Check
act_0010.raw: CONTACTS SKILL You have such extensive and well-informed contacts you can make an Investigation check to gather information in only one minute, assuming you have some means of getting in touch with your contacts.

act_0011.subject: Check
act_0011.raw: Further Investigation checks to gather information on the same subject require the normal length of time, since you must go beyond your immediate network of contacts.

act_0012.subject: Action
act_0012.raw: DAZE SKILL, RANKED (2) You can make a Deception or Intimidation check as a standard action (choose which skill when you acquire the advantage) to cause an opponent to hesitate in combat.

act_0013.subject: Action
act_0013.raw: Make a skill check as a standard action against your tar- get’s resistance check (the same skill, Insight, or Will de- fense, whichever has the highest bonus).

act_0014.subject: Effect
act_0014.raw: The limits of power flexibility in MUTANTS & MASTERMINDS are deliberately set by Variable effects, the use of extra effort, and hero points.

act_0015.subject: Character
act_0015.raw: However, generally speaking, these characters have certain abilities they use all the time (powers they have acquired with power points) and “stunts” they only do from time to time, essentially power stunts performed with extra effort (and possibly hero points).

act_0016.subject: Ability
act_0016.raw: most powers in the game have the potential to do “stunts” via extra effort, so the “variability” of Magic seen in the comics is already built-in to the system, with some costs to control it, without having to give players carte blanche to duplicate any effect in the game at will (which is just likely to slow things down and cause game balance issues).

act_0017.subject: Check
act_0017.raw: The target loses power points from the affected trait equal to the difference between the check result and the DC, up to a maximum of the Weaken rank.

act_0018.subject: Check
act_0018.raw: Multiple failed resistance checks against a Weaken effect are cumulative, up to a maximum of the Weaken rank, at which point the effect cannot weaken the trait further.

act_0019.subject: Ability
act_0019.raw: Any further uses of Weaken on the subject have no effect until the ability recovers to a rank of at least –5.

act_0020.subject: Character
act_0020.raw: For example, just because a Weaken Damage effect is possible doesn’t mean a character should be able to cause guns to do less damage;

act_0021.subject: Character
act_0021.raw: In the event that a character has more than one headquar- ters, such as hidden bases scattered around the world, the others are treated as Alternate Equipment:

act_0022.subject: Power
act_0022.raw: Such elementals may have Sustained powers and possibly the Activation Flaw if the transformation takes time or effort.

act_0023.subject: Effect
act_0023.raw: Alternately, the elementals who are considered Embodiments (as rolled on the Abilities) may have Innate forms that can- not be turned off even by nullification effects.

act_0024.subject: Perception
act_0024.raw: Requires Ambient Plant-life • 1 point • Plant Perception:

act_0025.subject: Character
act_0025.raw: Even then, dying characters have opportunities to stabilize and stave off death.

act_0026.subject: Attack
act_0026.raw: Bonuses to Toughness protect against slam attack dam- age normally.

act_0027.subject: Attack
act_0027.raw: The attacks to be combined must have the same effect and resistance and be within 5 ranks of each other.

act_0028.subject: Attack
act_0028.raw: So attacks all doing Damage against Toughness can combine, but not with a Mental Blast, for example, which is a Damage effect, but resisted by Will rather than Toughness.

act_0029.subject: Attack
act_0029.raw: Each attacker makes an attack check against the target’s defense.

act_0030.subject: Attack
act_0030.raw: those attacks simply miss and have no effect.

act_0031.subject: Damage
act_0031.raw: Objects, having no Stamina, do not recover from damage unless they have an effect like Regeneration.

act_0032.subject: Attack
act_0032.raw: Thus, foes with Visual Conceal- ment (the most common accurate sense) can be quite effective against characters relying on perception range attacks, unless the attacker has an unusual accurate sense to circumvent the Concealment.

act_0033.subject: Check
act_0033.raw: At the Gamemaster’s discretion, a successful Perception check to accurately locate a target with an acute sense may al- low you to use perception range effects on that target;

act_0034.subject: Effect
act_0034.raw: however, the target still benefits from concealment, granting a +5 circumstance bonus to resistance against the effect.

act_0035.subject: Damage
act_0035.raw: The effect a hit with the weapon causes, typically Damage, although some modern melee weapons have other effects.

act_0036.subject: Effect
act_0036.raw: The effect has the normal cost given in the Powers chapter.

act_0037.subject: Ability
act_0037.raw: The effect may also have certain descrip- tors, such as bludgeoning or slashing, for defining things like resistance or vulnerability to certain effects.

act_0038.subject: Character
act_0038.raw: Characters pay this cost from their equipment points to have a weapon of this type as part of their regular equipment.

act_0039.subject: Character
act_0039.raw: As a mental sensory effect, Mind Reading has a degree of subtlety, only noticeable to the subject or to characters with an appropriate mental sense, such as Mental Awareness (see the Senses effect).

act_0040.subject: Attack
act_0040.raw: Applied to Ranged Mind Reading, Close Mind Read- ing requires a close attack check to touch an unwilling tar- get and physical contact throughout the effect’s duration;

act_0041.subject: Attack
act_0041.raw: Ranged Mind Reading requires a ranged attack check in addition to the effect’s normal resistance check.

act_0042.subject: Ability
act_0042.raw: So when all else fails, just have a player make a check with the most appropriate trait (ability, skill, or power).

act_0043.subject: Power
act_0043.raw: Likewise, if a power level 10 hero is going to take out a PL 3 thug, you don’t have to make all the rolls.

act_0044.subject: Action
act_0044.raw: Dazed and staggered results have no real effect on inanimate targets, since they do not take actions.

act_0045.subject: Toughness
act_0045.raw: Equipment has Toughness based on its material.

act_0046.subject: Toughness
act_0046.raw: Devices have a base Toughness equal to the total points in the device divided by 5 (rounded down, minimum of 1).

act_0047.subject: Damage
act_0047.raw: Objects, having no Stamina, do not recover from damage unless they have an effect like Regeneration.

act_0048.subject: Attack
act_0048.raw: RANGE An attack has one of three ranges:

act_0049.subject: Attack
act_0049.raw: At medium range (up to rank x 50 feet), the attack check has a –2 circumstance modi- fier.

act_0050.subject: Attack
act_0050.raw: At long range (up to rank x 100 feet), the attack check has a –5 circumstance modifier.

act_0051.subject: Attack
act_0051.raw: MATERIAL TOUGHNESS Paper Soil Glass Ice Rope Wood Stone Iron Reinforced Concrete Steel Titanium Super-alloys RECOVERY 0 0 1 1 1 3 5 7 8 9 15 20+ Perception and Area effects do not require attack checks, they automatically affect a given target or area (see the Area extra in the Powers chapter).

act_0052.subject: Attack
act_0052.raw: To attack a target, you first have to have some idea of where to aim your attack.

act_0053.subject: Effect
act_0053.raw: POWERS -1 OR -2 COST PER RANK An effect has a range of close, ranged, or perception.

act_0054.subject: Character
act_0054.raw: So long as the character has power points invested in the device, it can be repaired, eventually.

act_0055.subject: Ability
act_0055.raw: Captain Thunder has the ability to hurl thunderbolts that shock their targets with electric- ity and deafen them with powerful claps of thunder.

act_0056.subject: Action
act_0056.raw: The combined effect costs 4 points per rank.Since both effects are ranged and require a standard action to use, so does the combined effect.

act_0057.subject: Check
act_0057.raw: Since Damage requires a Toughness check and Affliction requires a Dodge check, the target checks against them sepa- rately, making a Toughness resistance check against the damage of the lightning and a Dodge check to avoid being deafened by the thunder.

act_0058.subject: Attack
act_0058.raw: To use a Multiattack against a single target, make your attack check normally.

act_0059.subject: Attack
act_0059.raw: If an Impervious Resistance would ignore the attack be- fore any increase in the DC, then the attack still has no ef- fect as usual;

act_0060.subject: Attack
act_0060.raw: So making a Multiattack against five targets is a –5 penalty to each attack check.

act_0061.subject: Check
act_0061.raw: the target must make a resistance check against an effect rank equal to your Penetrating rank.

act_0062.subject: Effect
act_0062.raw: So, if a rank 4 (Pene- trating 2) effect hits a target with Impervious 9, the target must resist a rank 2 effect (equal to the Penetrating rank).

act_0063.subject: Effect
act_0063.raw: If the effect were rank 6, the target would have to resist the full effect anyway, since its rank is greater than half the Impervious rank.

act_0064.subject: Damage
act_0064.raw: PRECISE FLAT • 1 POINT You can use a Precise effect to perform tasks requiring delicacy and fine control, such as using Precise Damage to spot-weld or carve your initials, Precise Move Object to type or pick a lock, Precise Environment to match a par- ticular temperature exactly, and so forth.

act_0065.subject: Ability
act_0065.raw: The GM has final say as to what tasks can be performed with a Precise effect and may require an ability, skill, or power check to deter- mine the degree of precision with any given task.

act_0066.subject: Action
act_0066.raw: REACTION +1 OR +3 COST PER RANK Each effect has a default action required to use it:

act_0067.subject: Action
act_0067.raw: Take a stan- dard action and choose an ally in your line of sight, who receives the benefits of cover against enemies in your line of sight and in range of your Multiattack.

act_0068.subject: Ability
act_0068.raw: For the ability to choose to have some triggering cir- cumstances activate the effect and some not, apply the Selective modifier as well.

act_0069.subject: Attack
act_0069.raw: For example, a “soft” fighting style focusing primarily on defense might include the advantages Defensive Attack, Improved Defense, Improved Trip, and Instant Up.

act_0070.subject: Attack
act_0070.raw: A “hard” fighting style focused on offense might include All-out Attack, Improved Critical, Improved Smash, Power Attack, and Startle for a fierce kiai shout!

act_0071.subject: Action
act_0071.raw: DIEHARD GENERAL When your condition becomes dying (see Conditions in the Action & Adventure chapter) you automatically stabilize on the following round without any need for a Fortitude check, although further damage—such as a fin- ishing attack—can still kill you.

act_0072.subject: Check
act_0072.raw: EVASION COMBAT, RANKED (2) You have a +2 circumstance bonus to Dodge resistance checks to avoid area effects (see the Area extra in the Powers chapter).

act_0073.subject: Defense
act_0073.raw: Abilities 28 + Powers 0 + Advantages 6 + Skills 18 + Defenses 8 = 60 SWAT (Special Weapons And Tactics) squads are made up of police officers with special training in squad-level tactics and weapon-use.

act_0074.subject: Attack
act_0074.raw: Insubstantial 4 Eidetic Memory, Ranged Attack 5 SKILLS Investigation 2 (+7), Perception 5 (+6), Persuasion 4 (+4), Technology 5 (+10), Vehicles 2 (+5) OFFENSE INITIATIVE +3 Ranged +8 Unarmed +9 Ranged, Damage * Close, Damage 11 * Damage bonus depends on the option chosen under Powers.

act_0075.subject: Action
act_0075.raw: A character has an allotment of actions, used during that character’s turn.

act_0076.subject: Character
act_0076.raw: Base initiative bonus is equal to the character’s Agility rank.

act_0077.subject: Character
act_0077.raw: Many characters have ad- vantages or powers that modify their initiative, such as Improved Initiative.

act_0078.subject: Character
act_0078.raw: If two characters have the same initiative result, they act in order of highest Dodge bo- nus first, then highest Agility and highest Awareness.

act_0079.subject: Character
act_0079.raw: Your base form has the same abilities, powers, advantages, skills, defenses, and totals as the summoned characters, but with the addition of the Summon Twin or Summon Triplets power.

act_0080.subject: Power
act_0080.raw: A Weather Controller’s powers are difficult to hide, often resulting in a public identity.

act_0081.subject: Effect
act_0081.raw: If you’re interested in playing a hero who has both telekinetic and telepathic powers, take the Telepa- thy power, then take the Telekinesis power as the first power in an array and roll for (or choose) three alternate effects off whichever lists you want.

act_0082.subject: Power
act_0082.raw: Psychics often keep their identities and the fact that they have powers a secret from the rest of the world.

act_0083.subject: Character
act_0083.raw: In this case, one character (usually the one with the high- est bonus) is considered the leader of the effort and makes the check normally, while each helper makes the same type of check using the same trait(s) against DC 10.

act_0084.subject: Attack
act_0084.raw: Attack Check = d20 + attack bonus + modifiers vs.

act_0085.subject: Check
act_0085.raw: Resistance Check = d20 + defense bonus + modifiers vs.

act_0086.subject: Action
act_0086.raw: Free actions include things like talking (heroes and villains always find time to say a lot in the middle of a fight), drop- ping something, ending the use of a power, activating or maintaining some other powers, and so forth.

act_0087.subject: Check
act_0087.raw: SKILLS 121 ceed or fail, a target’s true attitude towards you generally becomes hostile after you attempt an Intimidation check, even if they go along with you for the moment.

act_0088.subject: Check
act_0088.raw: Compare your check result against a single resistance check made by the GM for the entire group.

act_0089.subject: Check
act_0089.raw: Your Intimidation check must have the same effect on every member of the group (that is, you cannot demor- alize some and coerce others, for example).

act_0090.subject: Check
act_0090.raw: Rocky has no interest in hurting a bunch of kids, so he bellows, “Get outta the way or I’ll knock your blocks off!” and his player decides to use Rocky’s routine Intimi- dation check of 18 to attempt to coerce the entire group of minions into moving out of his way.

act_0091.subject: Check
act_0091.raw: The GM may make In- vestigation checks for you in secret, so you do not know exactly what you have found, or if you may have missed something.

act_0092.subject: Power
act_0092.raw: They have powers reflecting their elemental compo- sition, as well as control and mastery over that element.

act_0093.raw: Dazed and Visually Impaired, Stunned and Visually Disabled), Extra Condition, Limited Degree • 1 point DEFENSES DODGE +7 PARRY +5 FORTITUDE +4 TOUGHNESS +0 WILL +8 ABILITIES POWERS 68 7/0* SKILLS DEFENSES 28/35* TOTAL 23 24 150 *If you rolled Gadgeteer on the Powers/Equipment table, then you have Powers 7 and Advantages 28, otherwise you have Powers 0 and Advantages 35.

act_0094.subject: Check
act_0094.raw: The target has a –1 circumstance penalty to further resistance checks against damage.

act_0095.subject: Check
act_0095.raw: The target is dazed until the end of their next turn and has a –1 circumstance penalty to further checks against damage.

act_0096.subject: Check
act_0096.raw: The target is staggered and has a -1 circumstance penalty to further checks against damage.

act_0097.subject: Check
act_0097.raw: The circumstance penalties to Toughness checks are cu- mulative, so a target who fails three resistance checks against Damage, each with one degree of failure, has a total –3 penalty.

act_0098.subject: Check
act_0098.raw: If an incapacitated target fails a resistance check against Damage, the target’s condition shifts to dying.

act_0099.subject: Check
act_0099.raw: A dying tar- get who fails a resistance check against Damage is dead.

act_0100.subject: Power
act_0100.raw: So a power level 11 hero who has Duplication 8 creates a power level 8 “duplicate” with (8 x 15) 120 power points and proportionately lower-ranked traits.

act_0101.subject: Action
act_0101.raw: Dazed and staggered results have no real effect on inanimate targets, since they do not take actions.

act_0102.subject: Effect
act_0102.raw: the tar- get can perceive the use of the effect, but not its results:

act_0103.subject: Action
act_0103.raw: The action required to use the combined effects is the longest of its components and they use a single attack check (if one is required) and resistance check (if both effects use the same type of check).

act_0104.subject: Check
act_0104.raw: If the effects have different resistances, targets check against each effect separately.

act_0105.subject: Character
act_0105.raw: (Choose One) 6 Take the skills listed above, then choose between Set I or Set II below (you may wait to make this choice until after you know what weapon your character uses), then roll on the Background, Mental, and Physical Skills tables as directed.

act_0106.subject: Action
act_0106.raw: ACTION & ADVENTURE though you can move (move action) and then attack (stan- dard action) or attack and then move, you cannot move half your distance, attack, and then move the other half unless you have some special trait that allows you to do so.

act_0107.subject: Action
act_0107.raw: • Make any necessary resistance checks to recover You can use extra effort in order to take an additional standard or move action on your turn (see Extra Effort on page 19).

act_0108.subject: Check
act_0108.raw: It can involve several checks of the same trait, or multiple traits, such as a Persuasion check to get the heroes on good terms with the King of Atlantis, an Expertise check to explain the particular dan- ger the kingdom faces, and an Insight check to realize the king’s advisor has something other than the best interests of the kingdom and royal family at heart.

act_0109.subject: Check
act_0109.raw: It allows a Will resistance check to overcome the effect, denying you the defense bonus against that op- ponent (and applying this flaw to the effect).

act_0110.subject: Attack
act_0110.raw: For example, a Damage effect might involve whirling blades an attacker can avoid with a successful Dodge resistance check, circumventing the need for a Tough- ness check against the damage.

act_0111.subject: Check
act_0111.raw: Similarly a Weaken effect based on a poison dart might add a Toughness check to see if the dart penetrates the target’s skin in addition to making the usual Fortitude check against the effect.

act_0112.subject: Target
act_0112.raw: Success means the target has managed to avert his eyes, cover his ears, etc.

act_0113.subject: Check
act_0113.raw: Otherwise the effect works normally and the target makes the usual resistance check against it, if any.

act_0114.subject: Check
act_0114.raw: This provides a +10 bonus to resistance checks against the effect, but gives others partial concealment from that sense.

act_0115.subject: Effect
act_0115.raw: To give a target additional resis- tance to a sensory effect, use the Resistible flaw.

act_0116.subject: Check
act_0116.raw: Failure includes missing an at- tack check, or the target successfully resisting the effect.

act_0117.subject: Damage
act_0117.raw: Typical side effects include Affliction, Damage, or Weaken, or the base effect itself (it essentially rebounds and affects you instead).

act_0118.subject: Attack
act_0118.raw: The Side Effect does not require an attack check and only affects you, although the GM may permit some Side Effects with the Area modi- fier on a case-by-case basis.

act_0119.subject: Ability
act_0119.raw: Take away the device, and the wielder loses the ability to use those powers.

act_0120.subject: Character
act_0120.raw: Characters who want to have and use a device on a regular basis have to pay power points to have it, just like having any other power.

act_0121.subject: Effect
act_0121.raw: EFFECT Use Insight to learn an opponent’s combat capabilities.

act_0122.subject: Check
act_0122.raw: Re-roll a failed check against a hazard once.

act_0123.subject: Check
act_0123.raw: Circumstance bonus to checks against a type of opponent.

act_0124.subject: Ability
act_0124.raw: Each ability rank costs 2 power points, so Jon has spent 68 of his 150 points, just over a third.

act_0125.subject: Power
act_0125.raw: Fortunately, Jon has decided his hero doesn’t really have any powers, relying on skills, advantages, and equipment.

act_0126.subject: Defense
act_0126.raw: Jon has spent a total of (9 + 6 + 6 + 5) or 26 points on defenses, about half of what he has left.

act_0127.subject: Power
act_0127.raw: Tough- ness can only be improved using advantages and powers, and his hero doesn’t have any powers.

act_0128.subject: Defense
act_0128.raw: His hero has Toughness 3 from his Stamina and his Toughness defense can be up to 6, given his Dodge and Parry of 14.

act_0129.subject: Power
act_0129.raw: Since Jon doesn’t want his hero to have any powers, he decides it’s time to look at advantages.

act_0130.subject: Character
act_0130.raw: Characters with the Inventor and Artificer advantages can create temporary devices for use in an adventure.

act_0131.subject: Character
act_0131.raw: Gamemasters may require characters to spend a hero point to make temporary use of a device that doesn’t be- long to them, similar to performing a power stunt without suffering fatigue.

act_0132.subject: Attack
act_0132.raw: A battlesuit with an array of weapons may have a primary attack effect and several others as Alternate Effects (see the Alternate Effect modifier in the Powers chapter).

act_0133.subject: Power
act_0133.raw: Costumes may have other properties and can even be the source of a hero’s powers, such as in the case of battlesuits (previ- ously).

act_0134.subject: Power
act_0134.raw: POWERS 143 In some settings, the Gamemaster may require certain descriptors for all powers.

act_0135.subject: Character
act_0135.raw: For ex- ample, if all characters with powers are mutants, then all powers have the “mutant” descriptor by definition, un- less the player comes up with a good explanation why they should not.

act_0136.subject: Power
act_0136.raw: If all superhumans are psychic mutants, then all powers have both the “psychic” and “mutant” descriptors.

act_0137.subject: Character
act_0137.raw: A character who breaks this guideline—say the one alien in a setting where all powers are otherwise mutant in origin—might have a Benefit (unusual origin) or face certain complica- tions, possibly both.

act_0138.subject: Action
act_0138.raw: Attack effects require a standard action to use.

act_0139.subject: Action
act_0139.raw: Control effects require a standard action to initiate, but can then usually be sustained.

act_0140.subject: Check
act_0140.raw: Control effects used against unwilling targets usually require an at- tack check and allow a resistance check, the same for the hazards they are capable of causing, such as creating intense cold or dropping a heavy object on someone.

act_0141.subject: Action
act_0141.raw: Persuading someone is at least a standard action, usually quite a bit longer.

act_0142.subject: Attack
act_0142.raw: The he- roes try to convince the king further, but any ad- ditional checks need at least the same degree of success as the first to get his attitude to favorable, where he is willing to call off the attack, and more than one degree of failure on any check moves his attitude to hostile, where he orders the intruders arrested and the attack to begin at once!

act_0143.subject: Action
act_0143.raw: Dexterity You’re trained with a particular type of ranged attack, giving you a bonus to your attack checks with it equal to your skill rank (see Attack Check in The Basics and in the Action & Adventure chapter).

act_0144.subject: Attack
act_0144.raw: For a broader bonus to attack checks that is less than simply raising Dexterity rank, see the Ranged Attack advantage in the Advantages chapter.

act_0145.subject: Power
act_0145.raw: The Mystic commands incredible super- natural powers, but sometimes those powers get out of control, or awaken sleeping horrors, or cause other unintended consequences that have to be dealt with.

act_0146.subject: Power
act_0146.raw: Mystics often have the Complication that prevents them from using their powers when they can’t move and/or speak to cast their spells.

act_0147.subject: Power
act_0147.raw: SUPER-SHIELDS Just as power armor is a device version of otherwise or- dinary equipment armor, some heroes (and, less often, villains) have shield devices providing them with great- er benefits than an ordinary shield.

act_0148.subject: Damage
act_0148.raw: A hero able to hurl a shield at foes can even have a Ranged Damage effect with it.

act_0149.subject: Effect
act_0149.raw: Vehicles can even have power effects of their own.

act_0150.subject: Ability
act_0150.raw: Con- structs tend to be physically powerful, resilient, and often have some sort of ranged attack that’s either a weapon or innate ability granted by their inhuman form.

act_0151.subject: Action
act_0151.raw: SWIMMING DC +5 +5 +5 MODIFIERS Rescuing another character who cannot swim Rough or choppy water +1 speed rank (up to your full ground speed) +10 Stormy or turbulent water Fighting You’re trained with a particular type of close attack, giv- ing you a bonus to your attack checks with it equal to your skill rank (see Attack Check in The Basics and in the Action & Adventure chapter).

act_0152.subject: Defense
act_0152.raw: So at Shrinking 12, you are size rank –5 (about 6 inches tall), and have a +6 bonus to active defenses and +12 Stealth bonus, but –3 Strength, –1 speed, and –6 Intimida- tion penalties.

act_0153.subject: Effect
act_0153.raw: If you have this extra, you might also acquire a Dimensional Travel effect allowing you to shift into a sub-atomic “universe” or simi- lar realm.

act_0154.subject: Effect
act_0154.raw: A summoned minion is limited to a Power Level equal to the rank of the Sum- Summon is a useful effect;

act_0155.subject: Action
act_0155.raw: Second, Gamemasters may wish to have groups of minions use team checks (see Team Checks in The Basics chapter) rather than rolling their actions separately.

act_0156.subject: Effect
act_0156.raw: POWERS 189 Arrays—collections of Alternate Effects—are one of the more complex and important constructs in MUTANTS & MASTERMINDS and require some special care in terms of their creation and use.

act_0157.subject: Character
act_0157.raw: It’s based on the assumption that a wide range of powers has a diminishing return in terms of value, since characters can only use so many effects at once.

act_0158.subject: Character
act_0158.raw: so you do not have to fill up character sheets with minor Alternate Effects a hero will rarely ever use.

act_0159.subject: Power
act_0159.raw: Arrays should have some unifying theme beyond “all the powers I want my hero to have,” and Gamemasters should feel free to veto inap- propriate arrays lacking a strong theme.

act_0160.subject: Power
act_0160.raw: The SABER battlesuit has an array of weapons drawing on a common power-source, and therefore not usable at the same time.

act_0161.subject: Effect
act_0161.raw: The base effect (Create) has a 1-point modifier to make it Dynamic, and each additional effect added to the array costs 2 points (1 for the Alternate Effect, 1 to make it Dynamic as well).

act_0162.subject: Effect
act_0162.raw: An effect with this modifier has a different resistance than usual.

act_0163.subject: Effect

act_0164.subject: Effect

act_0166.subject: Character

act_0167.subject: Character
act_0167.raw: How often characters have to make Fortitude checks de- pends on the conditions.

act_0168.subject: Character
act_0168.raw: Characters can hold their breath for ten rounds (one min- ute) plus a number of rounds equal to twice their Stamina.

act_0169.subject: Character
act_0169.raw: Failure on the Fortitude check means the character becomes incapaci- tated .

act_0170.subject: Damage

act_0171.subject: Check

act_0172.subject: Damage

act_0173.subject: Check

act_0174.subject: Check
act_0174.raw: A Treatment skill check can provide a bonus on resistance checks against disease.

act_0178.subject: Character

act_0183.subject: Power
act_0183.raw: The Gamemaster also may choose not to award a power point for an adventure in which the heroes did especially poorly, such as failing to defeat a villain’s major scheme or al- lowing many innocent people to suffer harm they could have prevented.

act_0188.subject: Attack
act_0188.raw: Rank 1 requires a Perception check (DC 20) to detect that you are Insubstantial, while 2 ranks mean you look entirely normal in Insubstantial form (which may cause opponents to waste effort on you, not knowing you are immune to their attacks, for example).

act_0189.subject: Check

act_0190.subject: Effect
act_0190.raw: The target of this last effect may spend a hero point or use Luck to avoid having to re-roll.

act_0193.subject: Check

act_0195.subject: Attack

act_0197.subject: Character
act_0197.raw: GAMEMASTER GUIDELINES Your GM may have particular guidelines for characters in the game, such as not allowing certain powers or concepts or requiring particular descriptors.

act_0201.subject: Check

act_0204.subject: Effect

act_0208.subject: Action
act_0208.raw: Activation tak- ing less than a move action is not a flaw, although may qualify as a complication (see the Power Loss complica- tion for details).

act_0209.subject: Effect

act_0210.subject: Action

act_0211.subject: Effect

act_0250.raw: COMBAT If you take the defend action (see Defend in the Action & Adventure chapter) and successfully defend against a close weapon attack, you can make an attack against the attacker’s weapon immediately as a reaction.

act_0251.subject: Character
act_0251.raw: When encountering an individual, group, or organization for the first time, you can make an immediate Investigation or Persuasion skill check to see if your character has heard something about the subject.

act_0253.subject: Character
act_0253.raw: Halfway between skills and powers, advantages are minor benefits characters have, allowing them to do things oth- ers cannot.

act_0254.subject: Effect
act_0254.raw: A power may have just one effect or several, and you can apply various modifiers to the effects to change how they work, customizing them to get just the right power.

act_0255.subject: Effect
act_0255.raw: Power effects have ranks like abilities do, on a scale from 1 to 20 (sometimes more).

act_0256.subject: Effect
act_0256.raw: Unlike abilities, effects do not have ranks of less than 1, since the “average” is not having powers at all!

act_0263.raw: When the action starts hap- pening, such as when the heroes are staving off a disaster or fighting villains, time becomes more crucial and is broken down into action rounds, and the players generally have to make die rolls to see how their heroes do.

act_0267.raw: If your hero has the Swing- ing movement effect (see Powers), then this is auto- matic.

act_0269.subject: Check
act_0269.raw: So it all depends on the outcome of the Fighting check against the bad guy.

act_0270.subject: Power
act_0270.raw: Heroes who gained their powers through training may have rivals or foes who trained with them (see the Enemy and Rivalry complica- tions later in this chapter).

act_0271.subject: Character
act_0271.raw: For example, a super-powered mutant has the “mutant” descriptor, mean- ing the character may be detected by mutant-detection powers, affected by mutant-specific devices, and so forth.

act_0272.subject: Check
act_0272.raw: He rolls a 25 result on the check and, a min- ute later, Makeshift has a makeshift mind-shield he hopes will protect him from Gepetto’s power long enough to try and free his teammates from the vil- lain’s influence.

act_0273.subject: Character
act_0273.raw: Just because a character happens to own a cell phone, laptop computer, car, or a home does not mean the char- acter is expected to have ranks in the Equipment advan- tage.

act_0274.subject: Character
act_0274.raw: Broadly speaking, MUTANTS & MASTERMINDS characters are only expected to pay for adventuring equipment, which is to say items that have a direct impact on their roles as heroes.

act_0275.subject: Effect
act_0275.raw: “Jury-rigging” a ritual has the same effects as for an inven- tion.

act_0276.subject: Check
act_0276.raw: Spending a hero point allows the ritualist to skip the design check and perform the ritual in a number of rounds equal to its cost.

act_0277.subject: Check
act_0277.raw: Magic check against a DC equal of (15 + the ritual’s cost) is needed to successfully perform the ritual.

act_0278.subject: Effect
act_0278.raw: The GM has final say on whether or not a particular object is too complex for this effect.

act_0279.raw: This requires both an attack check against the target’s Dodge and a Dodge resistance check against the effect’s rank.

act_0280.subject: Character
act_0280.raw: A character able to call up animated snowmen, on the other hand, should have Summon, while a powerful “ice elemental” may very well have both effects!

act_0281.subject: Effect
act_0281.raw: Imposing conditions on the target other than just trapping them requires a separate effect, such as Affliction (see Afflic- tion in this chapter), which you may wish to acquire as an Alternate Effect of Create (see Alternate Effect in this chapter).

act_0282.subject: Check
act_0282.raw: The object inflicts damage equal to its Toughness, and targets get a Dodge check to evade the falling object.

act_0283.subject: Effect
act_0283.raw: If a created object needs to support weight—created as a bridge or to support a weakened structure, for exam- ple—it has an effective Strength equal to its rank.

act_0284.subject: Character
act_0284.raw: In the case of the Mimic, the GM may wish to put together note cards or some other quick reference to the powers of other characters whom the Mimic might wish to duplicate.

act_0285.raw: Reworking an ar- chetype for a different power level requires rebuilding the character to ensure everything matches the power level limits, but here are some quick guidelines for modifying an archetype on the fly.

act_0289.raw: Touching an unwilling subject requires an un- armed attack check against the subject’s Parry.

act_0290.raw: The effect works at a distance, limited by perception and path and requiring a ranged attack check against the subject’s Dodge defense.

act_0291.raw: Willing characters can forgo their resistance check against an effect, if they wish.

act_0293.raw: Successfully acting like who you appear to be may also require a Deception check with a DC equal to the observ- er’s Insight check, modified by familiarity if the observer knows the subject well, as mentioned previously.

act_0294.subject: Action
act_0294.raw: FEINTING You can use Deception as a standard action to mislead an opponent in combat.

act_0295.raw: If your Deception check succeeds, the tar- get is vulnerable against your next attack, until the end of your next round (see Vulnerable in the Conditions sec- tion of The Basics chapter).

act_0296.subject: Check
act_0296.raw: DC 10 15 20 TASK Basic message Complex message Message containing new or detailed information Anyone listening in on your innuendo can attempt a In- sight check against the message DC.

act_0297.raw: TRICKING You can use Deception to mislead an opponent into taking a potentially unwise action, such as trying to hit you while standing in front of an electrical junction box or at the edge of a precipice.

act_0298.subject: Attack
act_0298.raw: If your Deception check opposed by Decep- tion or Insight succeeds, your opponent is heedless of the potential danger and may hit the junction box or lose his balance and fall, if his attack against you fails.

act_0299.subject: Check
act_0299.raw: The GM sets DCs for specific tasks using the guidelines provided in The Basics chapter under Checks, keeping in mind that most job-related checks should be considered routine (see Routine Checks in that section).

act_0300.subject: Effect
act_0300.raw: However, like the Energy Control power, Magic can have a wide range of Alternate Effects, each a separate spell you have mastered.

act_0301.subject: Power
act_0301.raw: Magicians often have a Power Loss complication (see Complications in The Basics chapter):

act_0302.subject: Power
act_0302.raw: if they are un- able to freely speak and gesture to cast their spells, they cannot use Magic (or any related magical powers reli- ant on spellcasting).

act_0303.subject: Check
act_0303.raw: HEARING Make a check against a DC based on how loud the noise is or against an opposed Stealth check.

act_0304.subject: Check
act_0304.raw: SEEING Make a check against a DC based on how visible the ob- ject is or against an opposed Stealth check.

act_0305.subject: Character
act_0305.raw: Non-player characters each have an initial attitude to- wards you or your cause.

act_0306.subject: Character
act_0306.raw: A hero with the Concealment effect (see page 153) could use it to create a power called Blending, Blur, Cloak, Invisibility, Shadowmeld, or anything else appropriate to the character you wish to play.

act_0307.subject: Effect
act_0307.raw: It’s all a matter of how powerful the effect is and what modifiers have been placed on it to increase or decrease its performance.

act_0308.subject: Effect
act_0308.raw: Each effect of a power has a standard cost per rank.

act_0309.subject: Modifier
act_0309.raw: Modifiers have ranks, just like other traits.

act_0310.subject: Damage
act_0310.raw: It’s more inter- esting and clear to say a hero has a “Flame Blast” or “Light- ning Bolt” power than a generic “Damage effect.” “Flame” and “lightning” are descriptors for the Damage effect.

act_0311.subject: Effect
act_0311.raw: For example, a weather-controlling heroine has the following effects:

act_0312.subject: Damage
act_0312.raw: Her Damage effect is the power to throw lightning bolts, so it has the descriptor “lightning.” If a villain can absorb electricity, then his power works against the heroine’s Damage (since lightning is electrical in nature).

act_0313.subject: Power
act_0313.raw: Her Environment is the power to control the weather, giving it the descriptor “weather.” If the hero- ine’s power comes as a gift from the gods, it may also have the descriptor “divine” or “magical.” On the other hand, if it comes from her mutant genetic structure, then it has the descriptor “mutant.” A villain able to nullify mutant powers could potentially nullify all of the heroine’s powers!

act_0314.subject: Character
act_0314.raw: The players and Gamemaster should cooperate to apply the appropriate descriptors to characters’ powers and use com- mon sense when dealing with how the different descrip- tors interact.

act_0315.subject: Check
act_0315.raw: 1 point per rank You are immune to certain effects, automatically succeeding on any resistance check against them.

act_0316.subject: Damage
act_0316.raw: The sec- ond requires 5 ranks and only provides immunity to cold Damage effects.

act_0317.subject: Attack
act_0317.raw: Encourage players to use tactics, cleverness, power stunts, and hero points to deal with foes immune to their more conventional attacks.

act_0318.subject: Character
act_0318.raw: When the GM does so, you earn hero points you can use to enhance your character’s chances of success, amongst other things.

act_0319.subject: Action
act_0319.raw: (See Hero Points in The Basics and Action & Adventure chapters for more information.) Choose at least two complications for your hero:

act_0320.subject: Action
act_0320.raw: The GM can use your hero’s motivation to encourage certain actions, and enemies may do the same.

act_0321.subject: Power
act_0321.raw: Some such heroes see their powers as more of a curse than a blessing, but try to do some good with them while hoping and looking for a way to have a normal life.

act_0322.subject: Power
act_0322.raw: The responsibility of having great power can be a heavy burden but some heroes feel it is their duty to use the powers they’ve been given for the greater good.

act_0323.subject: Effect
act_0323.raw: POWERS 187 ply to at least one rank, and may apply to as many ranks as the effect has.

act_0324.subject: Damage
act_0324.raw: So the first 4 ranks of the Caliber’s Damage effect have the Burst Area modifier, costing 1 point more (or 3 per rank).

act_0325.subject: Check
act_0325.raw: Caliber makes a normal ranged at- tack check against the main target for his micro-rock- et launcher;

act_0326.subject: Damage
act_0326.raw: if he hits, the target has to resist Damage 7, and everyone within the area around the target re- sists Damage 4 (the Area Damage).

act_0327.subject: Damage
act_0327.raw: Even if he misses, the main target has to resist the Area Damage 4, since the micro-rocket explodes close by!

act_0328.subject: Modifier
act_0328.raw: MODIFIERS Some modifiers, rather than increasing or decreasing an ef- fect’s cost per rank, have a flat value in power points, noted as flat in the modifier’s header.

act_0329.subject: Power
act_0329.raw: Likewise, the Activation flaw has a flat value of –1 or –2 points, depending on how long the power takes to activate.

act_0330.subject: Effect
act_0330.raw: If the same effect also has a flat-value extra costing 2 points and a flat-value flaw worth –1 point, then you add 2 to the final cost and sub- tract 1, for a total of (8 points for the effect + 2 points for the flat extra – 1 point for the flat flaw) or 9 power points.

act_0331.subject: Effect
act_0331.raw: When an effect is used against a corporeal target, the effect’s rank is equal to the rank of this extra, up to a maximum of the effect’s full rank.

act_0332.subject: Character
act_0332.raw: Characters with lower ranks 1–3 of Insubstantial do not require this extra for their effects to work on the physical world, although they can apply it to their Strength rank to allow them to exert some Strength while Insubstantial.

act_0333.subject: Effect
act_0333.raw: Rank 1 allows the effect to work at half its normal rank against insubstantial targets (rounded down);

act_0334.subject: Effect
act_0334.raw: If the effect Affects Only Objects, working on objects but not on living creatures, it has a net modifier of +0.

act_0335.subject: Check
act_0335.raw: At the GM’s discretion, someone holding, carrying, or wear- ing an object can make a Dodge resistance check against the effect, representing pulling the object out of the way at the last moment.

act_0336.subject: Effect
act_0336.raw: If the effect Affects Only Others, and not you, it has a net modifier of +0.

act_0337.subject: Effect
act_0337.raw: FLAT • 1 POINT PER RANK An incorporeal being can use an effect with this extra on the corporeal world (see the Insubstantial effect descrip- FLAT • 1 OR 2 POINTS This modifier allows you to “swap-out” the effect for an en- tire other, alternate, effect!

act_0338.subject: Damage
act_0338.raw: For example, a Damage effect with the descriptor of “laser” might have a visual Dazzle as an Alternate Effect:

act_0339.subject: Effect
act_0339.raw: An Alternate Effect can have any rank, or combination of modifiers.

act_0340.subject: Effect
act_0340.raw: Alternate Effects may also have different de- scriptors, usually thematically linked, within reason.

act_0341.subject: Damage
act_0341.raw: This allows you to have two versions of a Damage effect, for example:

act_0342.subject: Character
act_0342.raw: Constructs are subject to the same power level limits as other characters and the Gamemaster should require con- structs controlled by the players to observe these limits.

act_0343.subject: Character
act_0343.raw: Non-player character constructs have their power level determined the same as other NPCs.

act_0344.subject: Strength
act_0344.raw: Constructs also have either no Intel- lect and Presence ranks or no Strength and Agility ranks.

act_0345.subject: Attack
act_0345.raw: Use Deception to redirect a missed attack at another target.

act_0346.subject: Action
act_0346.raw: Choose a target you can accurately perceive and have the GM make a secret Insight check for you as a free action, opposed by the target’s Deception check result.

act_0347.subject: Character
act_0347.raw: Ranks in a advantage are noted with a number after the advantage’s name, such as “Defensive Roll 2” (for a character who has taken two ranks in the Defensive Roll advantage), just like skill and power ranks.

act_0348.subject: Character
act_0348.raw: Characters normally have a –10 circumstance penalty to use interac- tion skills on animals, due to their Intellect and lack of lan- guage.

act_0349.subject: Expertise
act_0349.raw: ARTIFICER SKILL You can use the Expertise:

act_0350.subject: Power
act_0350.raw: POWERS jects cannot have their selectivity changed once they are created.

act_0351.subject: Modifier
act_0351.raw: They resist being moved with a Strength rank equal to the modifier rank.

act_0352.subject: Toughness
act_0352.raw: So you can create an object with volume rank 0 and Toughness equal to your Create rank, vice versa, or anywhere in be- tween, so long as the sum of the two ranks does not ex- ceed your Create rank.

act_0353.subject: Check
act_0353.raw: The target makes a Forti- tude or Will resistance check against your effect DC (choose one when you acquire the effect).

act_0354.subject: Check
act_0354.raw: The target automati- cally fails Perception checks involving the sense, and everything effectively has total concealment from that sense.

act_0355.subject: Effect
act_0355.raw: Multiple Dazzle effects against the same sense are cu- mulative.

act_0356.subject: Defense
act_0356.raw: Julia has 24 points left for Princess’ defenses and skills.

act_0357.subject: Power
act_0357.raw: Looking at Complications, Julia decides Princess has a boyfriend who was present when she gained her powers and therefore knows her secret identity.

act_0358.subject: Action
act_0358.raw: ACTION & ADVENTURE 245 A successful attack has some effect on the target.

act_0359.subject: Check
act_0359.raw: The effect has a rank, used to determine a difficulty class for the target’s resistance check.

act_0360.subject: Attack
act_0360.raw: Resistance Difficulty = effect rank + 10 The target of the attack makes a resistance check against the effect to determine what, if anything, happens.

act_0361.subject: Check
act_0361.raw: At the end of his turn, he makes a Fortitude resistance check against the effect’s DC to try and shake it off, but missed the check by 2.

act_0362.subject: Attack
act_0362.raw: A failed resistance check against an attack imposes one or more conditions on the target, depending on the type of effect and the degree of failure.

act_0363.subject: Action
act_0363.raw: The GM should use these as guidelines for dealing with unusual actions play- ers may choose for their characters, basing them on the existing action descriptions.

act_0364.subject: Attack
act_0364.raw: If you succeed, you don’t actu- ally hit or affect the opponent, but success grants your ally gains a +2 circumstance bonus on an attack check against that opponent or a +2 circumstance bonus to Defense against that opponent (your choice) until the end of your ally’s next turn.

act_0365.subject: Action
act_0365.raw: However, you are vulnerable while aiming and it requires a free action to maintain your aim before you make your attack.

act_0366.subject: Action
act_0366.raw: ATTACK With a standard action, you can make an attack check against any opponent within the attack’s range.

act_0367.subject: Attack
act_0367.raw: At the end of your movement, you perform a close attack against your opponent with a –2 circumstance penalty to the attack check.

act_0368.subject: Action
act_0368.raw: COMMAND Issuing a command to a character under your control— a minion or a thrall—requires a move action.

act_0369.subject: Action
act_0369.raw: If you want to issue different commands to different characters or groups, each one requires a move action (so you can issue two commands per round as two move actions).

act_0370.subject: Check
act_0370.raw: Make an op- posed check of your appropriate active defense versus 246

act_0371.subject: Attack
act_0371.raw: POWERS EXTRAS NAME COST Accurate 1 flat per rank +2 attack check bonus per rank Affects Corporeal 1 flat per rank Effect works on corporeal beings with rank equal to extra rank.

act_0372.subject: Effect
act_0372.raw: Alternate Resistance +0-1 per rank Effect uses a different resistance.

act_0373.subject: Effect
act_0373.raw: effects cannot have Alternate Effects, nor can they be Al- ternate Effects (since they can’t be turned on and off ).

act_0374.subject: Effect
act_0374.raw: An Alternate Effect can have a total cost in power points no greater than the primary effect.

act_0375.subject: Effect
act_0375.raw: So a rank 10 primary effect costing 2 points per rank, for a total of 20, can have any Alternate Effect with a cost of 20 power points or less.

act_0376.subject: Effect
act_0376.raw: This cost does not include the cost of the Alternate Effect modifier itself.

act_0377.subject: Effect
act_0377.raw: So if the 20-point power has 5 Alternate Ef- fects (making the final cost 25 points), each Alternate Ef- fect is still limited to a total value of 20 points (including any modifiers it may have), that of the base effect.

act_0378.subject: Effect
act_0378.raw: Essen- tially, each Alternate Effect has to have all of the others as Alternate Effects.

act_0379.subject: Action
act_0379.raw: ACTION & ADVENTURE DAMAGE A successful attack with a Damage effect requires the tar- get to make a Toughness resistance check.

act_0380.subject: Check
act_0380.raw: The target has a –1 circumstance penalty to further resistance checks against damage.

act_0381.subject: Check
act_0381.raw: The target is dazed until the end of their next turn and has a –1 circumstance penalty to further checks against damage.

act_0382.subject: Check
act_0382.raw: The target is staggered and has a -1 circumstance penalty to further checks against damage.

act_0383.subject: Check
act_0383.raw: The circumstance penalties to Toughness checks are cu- mulative, so a target who fails three resistance checks against Damage, each with one degree of failure, has a total –3 penalty.

act_0384.subject: Check
act_0384.raw: If an incapacitated target fails a resistance check against Damage, the target’s condition shifts to dying.

act_0385.subject: Check
act_0385.raw: A dying target who fails a resistance check against Damage is dead.

act_0386.subject: Character
act_0386.raw: Just like Alternate Equipment, characters may have mul- tiple vehicles.

act_0387.subject: Power
act_0387.raw: Luxury vehicles often also include extras such as power seats, leather uphol- stery, and a sunroof.

act_0388.subject: Power
act_0388.raw: Impervious Toughness 7, Removable (-1 point) • 16 points The Weapon Master is recognized for his dedication to perfecting the use of a signature weapon and is capable of holding his own against superpowered beings due to his uncanny skill and the technological or magical en- hancements made to his weapon.

act_0389.subject: Power
act_0389.raw: Those Powerhouses with the Prejudice complication often choose this mo- tivation and become a hero in order to earn a place in “normal” society.

act_0390.subject: Power
act_0390.raw: Due to their incredible powers, many Powerhouses become heroes because they feel as if they have a responsibility to do so.

act_0391.subject: Power
act_0391.raw: PSYCHIC Psychic heroes have a wide variety of psionic powers available to them, but they typically concentrate on one category of powers, be they telepathic or telekinetic in or- igin.

act_0392.subject: Attack
act_0392.raw: Luck Control normally requires no attack check;

act_0393.subject: Effect
act_0393.raw: Effectively the GM gains a “free” complication against you.

act_0394.subject: Check
act_0394.raw: Targets failing a Will resistance check against your effect DC first become dazed, then compelled, as they try to fight off your influence.

act_0395.subject: Check
act_0395.raw: Degrees of failure on resistance checks against Mind Control are cumulative.

act_0396.subject: Check
act_0396.raw: To use Mind Read- ing, make an opposed effect check against the result of the target’s Will check.

act_0397.subject: Check
act_0397.raw: If you can interact with your subject, a successful Decep- tion check against the target’s Insight check causes the subject to consciously think about a particular piece of in- formation you’re looking for, such as a password or name, allowing you to pluck it from the subject’s mind with sur- face thoughts contact.

act_0398.subject: Character
act_0398.raw: On the other hand, a not so bright (low Intellect) character may have great deal of common sense (high Awareness).

act_0399.subject: Ability
act_0399.raw: The key differences between Enhanced Abilities and normal ability ranks are Enhanced Abilities can be nullified (normal abilities cannot, see Nullify, page 173) and Enhanced Abilities can have power modifiers and be used for power stunts with extra effort (normal abilities cannot, see Extra Effort, page 19).

act_0400.subject: Ability
act_0400.raw: Enhanced Abilities and normal abilities have the same cost (2 power points per +1 ability rank).

act_0401.subject: Character
act_0401.raw: Another character can stabilize a dying character with a successful Treatment check (DC 15) or use of a Healing effect (see the Powers chapter).

act_0402.subject: Attack
act_0402.raw: Opponents receive a +5 circumstance bonus to close attack checks but a –5 penalty to ranged attack checks (effectively giving the prone character total cover against ranged attacks).

act_0403.subject: Attack
act_0403.raw: The standard tank has Tough- Impervious ness 12 and comes equipped with a can- non (Ranged Damage 10, Burst Area 6) and a heavy machine gun (Ranged Multiattack Damage 6).

act_0404.subject: Damage
act_0404.raw: Battleships have massive gun batteries (Ranged Damage 13, Burst Area 9) and heavy armor.

act_0405.subject: Attack
act_0405.raw: Fighter jets have machine guns (Ranged Multiattack Damage 6) and air-to-air missiles (Ranged Damage 11, Burst Area 8, Homing 6).

act_0406.subject: Damage
act_0406.raw: Bombers may have machine guns and missiles, but also have powerful bombs (Burst Area Damage 12 or higher) they can drop on targets.

act_0407.subject: Damage
act_0407.raw: Space cruisers have larger beam weapons (Ranged Dam- age 12) and often energy torpedoes (Ranged Damage 12, Burst Area 10, Homing 8).

act_0408.subject: Character
act_0408.raw: Characters who don’t have any offen- sive powers often rely on weapons to get the job done.

act_0409.subject: Damage
act_0409.raw: They typically have a Strength-based Damage effect (see the Damage effect in the Powers chapter), adding the wielder’s Strength rank to the weapon’s damage rank.

act_0410.subject: Strength
act_0410.raw: If a wielder exerts Strength greater than the weapon’s Toughness (4 for wooden weapons, 7 or 8 for metal weapons), the weapon breaks when it is used.

act_0411.subject: Character
act_0411.raw: Please note, the characters on pages 35-49 include some Advantages in italicized print.

act_0412.subject: Character
act_0412.raw: If you’re pressed for time, looking for some inspiration, or prefer to be surprised, go to page 54 and use the Quickstart Character Generator to create a power level 10 hero.

act_0413.subject: Effect
act_0413.raw: use the rules for other effects as guidelines on how to handle these situations, using Transform rank to determine resistance DCs.

act_0414.subject: Character
act_0414.raw: Keep in mind, however, that Transform has a sustained duration, which may affect how such “tricks” work (e.g., the trap disappears if the character is stunned, the suffo- cating gas dissipates unless the character concentrates each round to continue transforming it, etc.).

act_0415.subject: Effect
act_0415.raw: For an effect capable of wearing- down and eventually destroying objects, use Weaken Toughness instead.

act_0416.subject: Effect
act_0416.raw: Transforming living or otherwise animate beings as op- posed to inanimate matter requires an effect other than Transform.

act_0417.subject: Check
act_0417.raw: When dealing with non-living creatures (those ab- sent Stamina) capable of resistance checks (possessing Will) the GM may permit Transform to function like an Affliction against them.

act_0418.subject: Target
act_0418.raw: Transforming someone’s devices or equipment requires targeting them first:

act_0419.subject: Attack
act_0419.raw: So transforming a hand-held weapon like a gun requires an attack check and permits the wielder a Dodge re- sistance check with a +5 bonus (for a hand-held item).

act_0420.subject: Attack
act_0420.raw: Targeting a worn suit of armor requires an attack check and allows the wearer a Dodge resistance check (with no modifier for a large item).

act_0421.subject: Effect
act_0421.raw: So you cannot, for example, acquire Enhanced Trait as a Variable effect to improve a trait beyond its power level limit, or acquire effects or descriptors the Gamemaster has specifically banned from the series.

act_0422.subject: Effect
act_0422.raw: The GM has final say as to whether or not a particular use of a Variable effect is appropriate and may veto your allocations, if necessary.

act_0423.subject: Power
act_0423.raw: for the Du- plicator, use the totals listed for the appropriate Twin or Triplets, then add the points for the Summon power.

act_0424.subject: Power
act_0424.raw: He or she uses those powers to gain acceptance.

act_0425.subject: Ability
act_0425.raw: Outside of their Summon ability, many Summoners don’t have a lot of powers.

act_0426.subject: Power
act_0426.raw: They draw their powers from a mystical or infernal source, al- though some Supernatural Creatures have a scientific ori- gin or explanation for their powers.

act_0427.subject: Action
act_0427.raw: Once you have hit with a Concentration Weaken, so long as you continue to take a standard action each turn to maintain the effect, the target must make a new resistance check against it, with no attack check re- quired.

act_0428.subject: Modifier
act_0428.raw: Weaken with this modifier cannot have its ef- fects countered by another power (such as Restorative Healing) without the Persistent modifier;

act_0429.subject: Effect
act_0429.raw: A Weaken effect capable of reducing more than one trait at once can have this modifier, allowing you to choose which traits are affected, while not affecting others.

act_0430.subject: Target
act_0430.raw: Combined with Precise (previously), you can use an Area Weaken to selectively af- fect only certain traits of certain targets.

act_0431.subject: Effect
act_0431.raw: Modified Cost = base effect cost + extras - flaws If total flaws reduce an effect’s cost per rank to less than 1 power point, each additional –1 to cost per rank beyond that adds to the number of ranks of the effect you get by spending 1 power point on a 1-to-1 basis.

act_0432.subject: Effect
act_0432.raw: If that effect has a total of –2 in modifiers, it costs 1:1, or 1 power point per rank.

act_0433.subject: Check
act_0433.raw: Disarming an explosive also requires a Technology check.

act_0434.subject: Check
act_0434.raw: The GM makes your Technology check secretly so you don’t necessarily know right away if you have suc- ceeded.

act_0435.subject: Character
act_0435.raw: POWERS tures a character has to use and maintain rather than hav- ing them as passive traits requiring no effort whatsoever.

act_0436.subject: Character
act_0436.raw: The Feature effect is intended to round out various minor traits and abilities characters might have, but it is entirely optional and not meant to burden MUTANTS & MASTERMINDS character design with needless amounts of detail.

act_0437.subject: Character
act_0437.raw: The player can then choose whether or not to use earned power points to ac- quire the Feature as a regular part of the character’s traits later on.

act_0438.subject: Toughness
act_0438.raw: Protection, Sustained • 1 point per rank You can surround your body with a protective field of energy or force, providing you with a Toughness in- crease equal to your rank.

act_0439.subject: Character
act_0439.raw: SKILLS Choose the skill ranks you want your character to have and pay 1 power point per 2 total skill ranks.

act_0440.subject: Character
act_0440.raw: ADVANTAGES Choose the advantages you want your character to have and pay 1 power point per advantage or rank in a advan- tage.

act_0441.subject: Ability
act_0441.raw: Each ability, skill, advantage, power, and other trait has an associated power point cost.

act_0442.subject: Power
act_0442.raw: Once spent, power points cannot be re-allo- cated without the use of a particular power or the Game- master’s permission.

act_0443.subject: Defense
act_0443.raw: If the headquarters has features like Defense System and Security System, they also extend over the grounds (with fences, sensors, weapon emplacements, and so forth).

act_0444.subject: Character
act_0444.raw: An infirmary can provide treatment for a number of characters equal to the base’s power level at one time and it can be assumed to have the necessary facilities to handle any unusual physiology of the base’s owner(s).

act_0445.subject: Character
act_0445.raw: Characters can use the laboratory to perform research, study unusual phenomena (including many super-powers), and so forth.

act_0446.subject: Power
act_0446.raw: Someone who fought in the Second World War is likely to have a differ- ent worldview than a modern teenager who just acquired super-powers, to say nothing of an immortal who has seen civilizations rise and fall or a godlike being from the dawn of time.

act_0447.subject: Character
act_0447.raw: Aged characters are likely to have lower physical abil- ity ranks, for example, while younger ones may have fewer skill ranks (having had less time to train in various skills).

act_0448.subject: Power
act_0448.raw: Although heroes spend a lot of time fighting crime and us- ing their powers to help others, most also try to find time to have lives of their own.

act_0449.subject: Ability
act_0449.raw: An artificial intelligence or magically created Construct may have been created to “do good” and pursues that goal to the best of its ability.

act_0450.subject: Attack
act_0450.raw: robots may take extra damage from electrical or magnetic attacks, ghosts or magical creatures from magical or holy at- tacks, and all Constructs may have a weakness to be- ing mind controlled by appropriate outside sources.

act_0451.subject: Attack
act_0451.raw: Percep- tion area effects neither require an attack check nor allow a Dodge resistance check, although targets still get a normal resistance check against the effect.

act_0452.subject: Action
act_0452.raw: The effect has close range and requires a standard action and an attack check to touch the subject.

act_0453.subject: Ability
act_0453.raw: For the ability to use both options simultaneously—to teleport a target and yourself at the same time, for example—take the effects as separate powers.

act_0454.subject: Target
act_0454.raw: Targets in other dimensions you cannot sense have total concealment from you.

act_0455.subject: Modifier
act_0455.raw: FLAT • 1 POINT PER RANK This modifier extends the distance over which a ranged ef- fect works.

act_0456.subject: Effect
act_0456.raw: The GM may set limits on the maximum Extended Range an effect can have;

act_0457.subject: Effect
act_0457.raw: The GM decides when and how a faded effect recovers, but it should generally occur outside of combat and take at least an hour’s time.

act_0458.subject: Attack
act_0458.raw: If your power’s mani- festation is damaged, make a resistance check against the attack’s damage rank, using your effect’s rank as the resistance check bonus.

act_0459.subject: Attack
act_0459.raw: For example, if a manifestation of a rank 10 effect is attacked for damage 12, you must make a resistance check against damage 12 with a +10 bonus (the effect’s rank) in place of your normal Toughness.

act_0460.subject: Attack
act_0460.raw: GRAB-BASED -1 COST PER RANK An attack effect with this flaw requires you to successfully grab a target before using the effect (see Grab, page 248).

act_0461.subject: Effect
act_0461.raw: Lamprey has a draining touch that is a Grab-Based Weaken Strength effect.

act_0462.subject: Attack
act_0462.raw: If his close attack check hits, the target makes a Dodge or Fortitude resistance check against Lamprey’s Strength.

act_0463.subject: Ability
act_0463.raw: For example, an attack effect that does less damage against targets with Protection (to represent a diminished ability to penetrate armor, for example) applies the Limited flaw to only those ranks that are ineffective.

act_0464.subject: Power
act_0464.raw: A Quirk is generally worth, at most, 2–3 power points, and many are simply 1-point flaws.

act_0465.subject: Power
act_0465.raw: On the other hand, a shapeshifter unable to change color (losing some of the power’s utility), or a telepath unable to lie while using Mental Communication, do have Quirks to their powers.

act_0466.subject: Power
act_0466.raw: Energy Controllers may suffer diminished use of their powers under descriptor-dependent con- ditions.

act_0467.subject: Effect
act_0467.raw: For instance, a Cold Controller may find his powers less effective in extreme heat, a Fire Controller may not be able to use his powers in an environment without oxygen (and therefore fuel to sustain his fires), a Sonic Controller may not be able to use his powers in a vacuum (where sound does not carry), and so forth.

act_0468.subject: Roll
act_0468.raw: Energy Controllers often have a rivalry with an Energy Controller who commands an opposed descriptor.

act_0469.subject: Character
act_0469.raw: This modifier allows you to have an alternate set of traits, essentially a complete alternate character you change into, one set of traits per rank in Metamorph.

act_0470.subject: Effect
act_0470.raw: If you have the Metamorph extra, then targets that resist your effect treat you as if you had your normal traits, and not those granted by your Metamorph form.

act_0471.subject: Effect
act_0471.raw: Your effective Strength for lifting and moving objects with this effect is equal to your rank.

act_0472.subject: Damage
act_0472.raw: Your effect can inflict damage, like an appli- cation of normal Strength with damage equal to its rank.

act_0473.subject: Attack
act_0473.raw: This includes damaging targets in grabs and making ranged “strike” attacks.

act_0474.subject: Power
act_0474.raw: Gadgets can be temperamental things, so it’s not unimaginable that sometimes a Gadgeteer’s devices might act up, run out of power, break, ex- plode, or otherwise cause problems for the hero.

act_0475.subject: Attack
act_0475.raw: Power level 10 heroes may have a balance of attack and effect, defense and resistance, or may go for being stronger on one side than the other, having great combat skill, but comparatively limited damage, for example, or great Toughness, but lowered defenses.

act_0476.subject: Power
act_0476.raw: Those lacking superhuman powers (such as the Raven) have amazing levels of skill and resources to draw upon while the superhuman types are often among the most capable in their particular area, often worthy of titles like “King” and “World’s Greatest”.

act_0477.subject: Attack
act_0477.raw: In areas where power level 12 heroes tip the balance of their combat capabilities, they can have amazing ranks in attack (like the Raven’s Close Combat skill) or defense (like Daedalus’ Toughness).

act_0478.subject: Ability
act_0478.raw: This includes un- trained skill modifiers using only ability rank, and so sets an effective limit on all abilities associated with skills.

act_0479.subject: Attack
act_0479.raw: If an effect allows a resistance check, but does not require an attack check, its effect rank cannot exceed the series power level.

act_0480.subject: Attack
act_0480.raw: TRADE-OFFS Note that the averaging effect of power level—the fact that all the traits it limits are paired with other traits—al- lows for a measure of “trade-off.” For example, attack bo- nus and effect rank added together cannot exceed twice the series power level, but this does not mean the two traits must themselves be equal, or that neither can be greater than the series PL.

act_0481.subject: Effect
act_0481.raw: Typically a Weaken effect against Strength, Agility, or Stamina (sometimes more than one), with Weaken Stamina potentially lethal, if the victim’s Stamina drops below –5.

act_0482.subject: Effect
act_0482.raw: Side-effects might include conditions like dazed, impaired, or hindered.

act_0483.subject: Power
act_0483.raw: Some powers may not have or need result descriptors;

act_0484.subject: Effect
act_0484.raw: it has an invented origin (someone designed and built it), a techno- logical source (it’s a technological device with a battery), uses a energy medium (an electrical shock), and results in an electrical overload of the tar- get’s nervous system (the result descriptor for its Affliction effect).

act_0485.subject: Action
act_0485.raw: ACTION & ADVENTURE NO COVER COVER Targets may also hide behind obstructions to gain cover against your attacks.

act_0486.subject: Effect
act_0486.raw: • Certain traits (like the Takedown advantage) are more effective against or specifically target minions.

act_0487.subject: Attack
act_0487.raw: Defense Class = defense + 10 Cover also grants a circumstance bonus to Dodge resis- tance checks against area effects equal to its penalty to attack checks, so long as the target has cover with respect to the origin point of the effect.

act_0488.subject: Check
act_0488.raw: So someone behind total cover also gains a +5 to Dodge checks against area effects.

act_0489.subject: Attack
act_0489.raw: • • Non-minions can make attack checks against min- ions as routine checks.

act_0490.subject: Attack
act_0490.raw: So a minion failing a So a hero with Parry 11 has a defense class of 21 (11 + 10) against close attacks.

act_0491.subject: Attack
act_0491.raw: If the same hero has Dodge 9, that is a defense class of 19 (9 + 10) against ranged attacks.

act_0492.subject: Dodge
act_0492.raw: So the aforementioned hero with Parry 11 and Dodge 9 would have ranks of Parry 6 and Dodge 5 while vulnerable.

act_0493.subject: Attack
act_0493.raw: What’s more, attackers can make attack checks against defenseless targets as routine checks (see Routine Checks in The Basics chapter), meaning a hit is guaranteed with an attack bonus of 0 or more, unless there are other modifiers affecting the check.

act_0494.subject: Ability
act_0494.raw: If Doctor Shock possessed the ability to have his aura only damage people and things he wants it to damage, he would need to have the Selec- tive modifier applied to the effect as well.

act_0495.subject: Effect
act_0495.raw: Nor- mally, you have no control over the results of such effects.

act_0496.subject: Attack
act_0496.raw: The “bounce” has no effect apart from changing the attack’s direction.

act_0497.subject: Check
act_0497.raw: The target gets the normal resistance check against the secondary effect.

act_0498.subject: Effect
act_0498.raw: For a degree of selectivity with non-resistible effects, use the Precise modifier.

act_0499.subject: Effect
act_0499.raw: SLEEP +0 COST PER RANK When this modifier is applied to an effect that causes the incapacitated condition, the effect leaves them asleep whenever it would normally render them incapacitated.

act_0500.subject: Attack
act_0500.raw: If an attack check is required, the attacker makes one, comparing the results against each target.

act_0501.subject: Action
act_0501.raw: SUSTAINED +0 COST PER RANK Applied to a permanent duration effect, this modifier makes it sustained duration, requiring a free action to use (rather than none, like other permanent effects).

act_0502.subject: Action
act_0502.raw: The drawback is the effect requires a free action each turn to maintain it, and being unable to do so means the effect shuts off.

act_0503.subject: Character
act_0503.raw: The Protection effect is permanent, meaning it always protects the character, but concentrating or trying harder does not make the effect more protective, nor can the character use it for power stunts.

act_0504.subject: Character
act_0504.raw: Choose or roll up another character using the tables in this book and use that as your summoned creature.

act_0505.subject: Power
act_0505.raw: Doc- tor Clock and Longhunter continue to battle his growing power, but Sanguine and Blackbird have been waiting a long time for another chance at true freedom and power, and they have a plan….

act_0506.subject: Check
act_0506.raw: Security cameras show only the statue vanishing in an instant, but a Technol- ogy skill check (DC 15) reveals that the footage has been al- tered somehow.

act_0507.subject: Character
act_0507.raw: Magic skill check (DC 20, the character receives a major advantage (+5) on the check if he or she also has the Ritualist advantage) spots sigils written in soot on the walls beneath the camera, magical writing that obscured the view of the camera.

act_0508.subject: Check
act_0508.raw: Salinas suspects a missing security guard might have been involved in the robbery, but an Investigation check (DC 15) finds the guard dead, stuffed into an air duct high up on a wall near an unlocked skylight.

act_0509.subject: Attack
act_0509.raw: Actually, the werewolves were already attacking the farm- ers working a small vegetable field, but now the heroes have arrived in the middle of the battle-in-progress and the werewolves—whom the heroes might hear the farmers call “Wolfen”—don’t hesitate to attack the newcomers as soon as they appear.

act_0510.subject: Check
act_0510.raw: The geography of Avison is rough- ly that of Angel Island (or at least, the island in the late eighteenth century.) However, due to the mys- tical effects shrouding and warping the island, any checks heroes make using their normal geographi- cal or historical familiarity are made at a -10.

act_0511.subject: Power
act_0511.raw: Similarly, any powers with magical descriptors operate as if they have one level (or one additional level) of the Improved Critical advantage.

act_0512.subject: Character
act_0512.raw: Doctor Clock is as friendly and curious as Longhunter is taciturn and guarded, but answers any questions the players have about them or Avison (see their character profiles and the Effects of Avison sidebar) in exchange for information from the heroes, specifically when it is in the outside world.

act_0513.subject: Power
act_0513.raw: My beloved Emerald Star—and my beloved wife—bought us the time we needed to craft a relic that would draw away the power of the beast-brothers and use it to seal Sanguine and Blackbird away in a prison stronger than any coffin.

act_0514.subject: Check
act_0514.raw: After the heroes recover the first artifact, a Perception check (DC 20, only DC 10 if a hero has Extended or Acute Hearing) allows them to hear chanting on the wind.

act_0515.subject: Action
act_0515.raw: Luckily, the heroes have both inadvertent allies and a distraction:

act_0516.subject: Attack
act_0516.raw: Avian members of the Sons of the Sky have also found the trees, and are mounting an aerial attack.

act_0517.subject: Check
act_0517.raw: the check begins with the Avians as Unfa- vorable, and can be improved to Indifferent if the heroes reveal that they know of Avison’s origins or have deduced that the Sons have come to rescue their bird brother- gods.

act_0518.subject: Check
act_0518.raw: An Insight or Perception check (DC 25, or DC 20 if the hero has Expertise:

act_0519.subject: Attack
act_0519.raw: Once on the run, they use pack tactics to protect each other and a couple locals they’ve captured, so if the heroes focus their attack on the monk with the Shroud, they concentrate their defense ac- cordingly.

act_0520.subject: Check
act_0520.raw: Rescuing a captured local from the clutches of a Blood Monk requires an opposed Grab check.

act_0521.subject: Attack
act_0521.raw: Even if the monks do manage to make it safely inside the mission, heroes still have another few minutes to marshal a second attack while the monks bar and block the gate… and then find that the other monks have locked the in- terior doors, leaving them in the courtyard to defeat the heroes or be defeated.

act_0522.subject: Strength
act_0522.raw: More to the point, a single hero is giv- en a chance to prove his or her strength in combat against the Wolfen champion.

act_0523.subject: Power
act_0523.raw: as such, the Wolfen also refuse to battle any hero whose powers or background have a connection to the Clans for the same reason they won’t accept Longhunter’s challenge.

act_0524.subject: Power
act_0524.raw: If a hero is over-confident, he uses the Claw to Nullify any movement powers they might have or Weaken their Agility or Strength, bringing them down into the fight (and the water).

act_0525.subject: Power
act_0525.raw: Sanguine uses your people’s power to bring his own followers through.

act_0526.subject: Attack
