act_0526.raw: TACTICS If given the opportunity, Blackbird uses her Ensorcel pow- er and attempts to bring one or more of the heroes under her control, but unleashes her Eldritch Fire if the heroes attack in pairs.

act_0527.subject: Power
act_0527.raw: Surreptitiously, Marmo- Set has located the most powerful remaining plague ava- tar, Choshech.

act_0528.subject: Attack
act_0528.raw: TACTICS The Bola uses his trick weapons to set up victims for his teammates’ attacks.

act_0529.subject: Power
act_0529.raw: As mentioned under Tactics, Weather Mistress may alter the environ- ment with her powers in other ways if it becomes advan- tageous to do so.

act_0530.subject: Action
act_0530.raw: ACTION Looking Glass is ready to talk, but she’s mostly interested manufacturing some state’s evidence to turn against the other Guild members, making her attitude Unfavorable.

act_0531.subject: Character
act_0531.raw: The Bola especially wants to spend some time with any female heroes present, so Persuasion skill checks attempted by female characters treat him as Favorable, and he defends against Deception checks with a major disadvantage (-5).

act_0532.subject: Power
act_0532.raw: Nearly drunk with newfound power, Marmo-Set has a greater than usual tendency to overreact:

act_0533.subject: Attack
act_0533.raw: To provide a clue for later, any magical attacks directed at Choshech should cause him visible hurt.

act_0534.subject: Effect
act_0534.raw: The circle of darkness hovering over the island (in truth, the remnant mystic energy that brought Marmo-Set to Earth-Prime) causes a shading effect.

act_0535.subject: Effect
act_0535.raw: Whether they accept or decline, the heroes’ decision has a major effect on the adventure’s outcome in Scene 4.

act_0536.subject: Action
act_0536.raw: If they give in to it, they are rewarded in Scene 4, and if they resist, they have the satisfaction of having not taken a morally suspect short- cut…just like true heroes.

act_0537.subject: Character
act_0537.raw: Player characters who first encounter Simian on the living level (location #6) in the warden’s office find him watching a TV newscast and a lone security monitor (the only pow- ered devices Simian has been able to restore, thus far).

act_0538.subject: Effect
act_0538.raw: A sharp blow there will neutralize him!” (See Marmo-Set’s Identity complication for full details.) Marmo-Set is still learning how best to use Choshech’s powers, but unlike Scene 2, his “guesses” as to what’s most effective against the heroes aren’t so devastatingly correct.

act_0539.subject: Ability
act_0539.raw: Absent abili- ties impose specific penalties greater than just a low (negative) rank in an ability.

act_0540.subject: Attack
act_0540.raw: An attack hits if the check result is equal to or greater than the target’s appropriate defense.

act_0541.subject: Action
act_0541.raw: An attack is usually a standard action and involves a check versus a target’s defense.

act_0542.subject: Character
act_0542.raw: The character cannot see, so everything effectively has full visual concealment from him.

act_0543.subject: Character
act_0543.raw: A controlled character has no free will;

act_0544.subject: Check
act_0544.raw: The use of one effect to block or eliminate another, requiring an opposed power check between the two effects.

act_0545.subject: Action
act_0545.raw: Countering normally requires a readied ac- tion, unless using a hero point to counter immediately as a reaction.

act_0546.subject: Attack
act_0546.raw: A critical hit does +5 damage over the attack’s normal damage, or you may add an effect, or use an alternate ef- fect.

act_0547.subject: Action
act_0547.raw: A dazed character is limited to free actions and a single standard action per round, although the character may use that action to perform a move, as usual.

act_0548.subject: Action
act_0548.raw: Maintaining an active illusion (such as a fighting creature) requires a standard action each round, but maintaining a static illusion (one that doesn’t move or interact) is only a free action.

act_0549.subject: Check
act_0549.raw: Illusion can alter a subject’s appearance, providing an essentially impenetrable disguise—at least until some- one makes a successful check to see through the illu- sion.

act_0550.subject: Ability
act_0550.raw: However, for just the ability to alter appearance, use the Morph effect, which is generally more effective than Illusion Limited to Appearance.

act_0551.subject: Attack
act_0551.raw: Although Illusion does not have a physical “manifestation” per se, it can apply this flaw, in which case a successful damaging attack on one of your illusions causes you to suffer damage, using the guidelines given in the description of the Feedback flaw.

act_0552.subject: Effect
act_0552.raw: So Illusions Resistible by Fortitude or Will have no effect on non-living targets, for example.

act_0553.subject: Character
act_0553.raw: 2 points per rank Keep in mind characters don’t get to make a resistance check to overcome an illusion unless they have reason to believe the illusion is not real.

act_0554.subject: Power
act_0554.raw: Smart illusionists keep the true nature of their powers secret, and smart Gamemasters require players to come up with something a bit more comprehensive than “I disbelieve!” to figure out when there are illusions at hand.

act_0555.subject: Character
act_0555.raw: A character can even have multiple bases of operation (see Alternate Headquarters later in this section).

act_0556.subject: Attack
act_0556.raw: Gen- erally, a combat simulator has a suite of equipment that can simulate any appropriate attack effect at a rank up to the HQ power level.

act_0557.subject: Effect
act_0557.raw: POWERS 175 use perception ranged effects via your Remote Sensing.

act_0558.subject: Attack
act_0558.raw: Your sen- sory-point is considered to have partial cover from attacks and you use your Remote Sensing rank as your Toughness defense against any successful attack.

act_0559.subject: Effect
act_0559.raw: Another effect might potentially have the flaw Remote-Sensing Depen- dent, such as an Affliction that targets only remote view- ers observing a target or an area as a means of blocking or deterring them.

act_0560.subject: Check
act_0560.raw: AWARENESS 1 RANK You can sense the use of effects of a particular descrip- tor with a successful Perception check (DC 10, with –1 to your check per 10 feet range).

act_0561.subject: Modifier
act_0561.raw: If you apply the Dimensional modifier to your Communication Link, it extends to other dimensions as well (see Dimen- sional under Power Modifiers for details).

act_0562.subject: Character
act_0562.raw: GADGETS & GEAR feature and require characters to pay for it if their charac- ters have such a costume.

act_0563.subject: Character
act_0563.raw: Otherwise characters have to make do with ordinary clothing (which may be damaged or destroyed when they use their powers).

act_0564.subject: Attack
act_0564.raw: Magical weap- ons may have greater damage bonuses or grant attack bonuses while magical armor imposes no penalties and provides greater protection.

act_0565.subject: Attack
act_0565.raw: A weapon device usually has one or more attack effects but may provide virtually any effect the player wants to include.

act_0566.subject: Attack
act_0566.raw: Weapons often have several different attacks as Alternate Effects.

act_0567.subject: Power
act_0567.raw: Use the guidelines in this chapter and in the Powers chapter to handle any new devices and their capabilities.

act_0568.subject: Power
act_0568.raw: Designing an invention requires an hour’s work per power point of the invention’s cost.

act_0569.subject: Check
act_0569.raw: Design Check = DC 10 + invention’s point cost If the check is successful, you have a design for the inven- tion.

act_0570.subject: Character
act_0570.raw: For this reason, the GM should make the design check secretly and only inform the player whether or not the character appears to have succeeded.

act_0571.subject: Power
act_0571.raw: Once the invention is complete, it is good for use in one scene, after which it breaks down or runs out of power.

act_0572.subject: Character
act_0572.raw: THEME Maybe the character has a theme or style suggesting a name:

act_0573.subject: Character
act_0573.raw: SOUND Some code-names don’t really have anything to do with a character’s powers or background—they just sound cool:

act_0574.subject: Power
act_0574.raw: They may hint at the hero’s powers or origin, or have nothing to do with them.

act_0575.subject: Power
act_0575.raw: ALIEN A hero may be a member of an alien race with unusual powers compared to humans.

act_0576.subject: Power
act_0576.raw: Either all members of the race have similar powers or particular conditions (lighter gravity, solar radiation, etc.) grant them powers while on Earth.

act_0577.subject: Power
act_0577.raw: An alien hero’s powers might even have an- other origin;

act_0578.subject: Character
act_0578.raw: It proved very popular and when we had the chance, we offered up the idea of including an updated version of it in the Hero’s Handbook to the fans and their response was a resound- ing, “Yes!” This updated version of the Quickstart Character Genera- tor includes clearer instructions, some new powers and options for some of the archetypes, and fixes to a couple of the archetypes to make sure they didn’t break any of the rules.

act_0579.subject: Character
act_0579.raw: Sure, this method removes a lot of choice from the pro- cess, but that speeds up character creation, since the player doesn’t have to make every single decision along the way (or even know the rules!).

act_0580.subject: Character
act_0580.raw: There are a few different ways to use the character gen- erator.

act_0581.subject: Character
act_0581.raw: After all, you’re here to have fun, so your main goal should be to make sure the player ends up with a character they want to play.

act_0582.subject: Roll
act_0582.raw: Sometimes that requires a number of rolls, sometimes just a few.

act_0583.subject: Effect
act_0583.raw: For ex- ample, the Battlesuit, Mystic, and Weather Controller all require a fair number of rolls to determine their Powers and Alternate Effects, while the Mimic, Shapeshifter, and Summoner require fewer rolls, because they have only a few powers.

act_0584.subject: Effect
act_0584.raw: All the Archetypes’ effects include names for their powers, such as the Battlesuit’s “Gravity Drivers,” which allow it to fly, but you’re welcome (encouraged, even!) to change the power’s name to something more to your liking.

act_0585.subject: Character
act_0585.raw: Descriptors are a simple way to customize your character and make it unique and interesting compared to all the other heroes out there.

act_0586.subject: Character
act_0586.raw: Once you have your character and paper, maybe moved some points around, and decided on your descriptors, you’re ready to go!

act_0587.subject: Attack
act_0587.raw: Make an attack check against the defender with a –2 penalty.

act_0588.subject: Attack
act_0588.raw: If your attack succeeds, make an opposed check of your attack’s damage against the defender’s Strength.

act_0589.subject: Action
act_0589.raw: Dropping to a prone position is a free action, although getting up requires a move action (see Stand).

act_0590.subject: Check
act_0590.raw: Make a check of your Athletics or Acrobatics against the routine check result of your opponent’s Strength or grab effect rank.

act_0591.subject: Dodge
act_0591.raw: Failure means you are surprised (although, if you have Uncanny Dodge, you are not vulnerable).

act_0592.subject: Check
act_0592.raw: Each additional time you apply this option, your range increases by an additional factor of 10, so 1 rank means you have a –1 to Perception checks per 100 feet, 2 ranks makes it –1 per 1,000 feet, and so on.

act_0593.subject: Character
act_0593.raw: Each time the character makes a resistance check against the ailment, you make a Treatment check.

act_0594.subject: Check
act_0594.raw: Routine tasks, such as ordinary operation of known vehi- cles, don’t require a check and may even be done untrained for some vehicles, particularly common ones like cars.

act_0595.subject: Modifier
act_0595.raw: Although Rook has the same result, he has a higher initiative modifier (+5) and so will go before the smugglers.

act_0596.subject: Action
act_0596.raw: Normally, a character wouldn’t be able to move, attack, and then move again as Rook does here, but he has the Move-by Action advantage, which allows him to split his movement in the round before and after his standard action.

act_0597.subject: Check
act_0597.raw: The successful Dodge checks make their Fortitude resistance checks against DC 11, while the failed checks resist DC 13 again:

act_0598.subject: Check
act_0598.raw: Knowing that well exceeds the smuggler’s Dodge defense of 12, rolls a Dodge resistance check, getting a 16, minus the impaired penalty from Princess’ Intimidation for a 14 vs.

act_0599.subject: Attack
act_0599.raw: The attack’s damage equals your power rank and the target makes a Toughness resistance check against it.

act_0600.subject: Check
act_0600.raw: RANGED 1 RANK You can use a sense that normally has no range (taste or touch in humans) to make Perception checks at range, with the normal –1 per 10 feet modifier.

act_0601.subject: Modifier
act_0601.raw: In fact, GMs looking to limit Pre- cognition and Postcognition may wish to require extra effort or hero points to use them, or require the Uncon- trolled modifier.

act_0602.subject: Effect
act_0602.raw: To affect the area of a sense itself, use the Extended and Radius traits of the Senses effect.

act_0603.subject: Effect
act_0603.raw: To extend the range of a sense it- self, use the Extended, Radius, and Ranged options of the Senses effect.

act_0604.subject: Power
act_0604.raw: As GM, you have discretionary power over hero points.

act_0605.subject: Power
act_0605.raw: This represents the experience and confi- dence the heroes have gained, along with other factors con- tributing to an increase in their abilities, skills, and powers.

act_0606.subject: Power
act_0606.raw: The Gamemaster also may choose not to award a power point for an adventure in which the heroes did especially poorly, such as failing to defeat a vil- lain’s major scheme or allowing many innocent people to suffer harm they could have prevented.

act_0607.subject: Check
act_0607.raw: By succeeding at a DC 10 Investigation check taking at least an hour, you get a feel for the major news and ru- mors in an area.

act_0608.subject: Check
act_0608.raw: The DC of the subject’s Stealth check to evade your notice is equal to the result of your Investigation check.

act_0609.subject: Modifier
act_0609.raw: Awareness MODIFIERS Every day since event (max modifier +10) Crime scene is outdoors Crime scene disturbed Crime scene highly disturbed Use this skill to notice and pick up on things.

act_0610.subject: Check
act_0610.raw: Discerning details—such as clearly hearing conversation or reading fine text—requires at least three degrees of success on the Perception check.

act_0611.subject: Check
act_0611.raw: In general, you have a –1 circumstance penalty to Percep- tion checks for every 10 feet between you and what you are +2 +5 +2 +5

act_0612.subject: Action
act_0612.raw: DC 5 15 20 30 TASK Lessen damage from a fall (–1 per degree) Acrobatic maneuver Move from prone to standing as a free action Contort to fit through a tight space ATHLETICS Strength Use Athletics for physical feats like climbing, jumping, rid- ing animal mounts, and swimming.

act_0613.subject: Attack
act_0613.raw: A ladder A knotted rope A rope An uneven surface, like a rock-face A rough surface, like a brick wall An air duct, chimney, or other area where you can brace against two opposite walls A corner where you can brace against perpendicular walls Climb of less than 10 feet total Surface slightly slippery Surface very slippery +1 speed rank (up to your full speed) Not vulnerable while climbing DC 0 5 10 15 20 –10 –5 –5 +2 +5 +5 +5 Since you can’t easily move to avoid attacks, you are vul- nerable while climbing unless you accept a +5 increase in the DC.

act_0614.subject: Action
act_0614.raw: Presence • Interaction You know how to use threats (both real and implied) to get others to do what you want.

act_0615.subject: Check
act_0615.raw: COERCING Make an Intimidation check, opposed by the target’s In- sight or Will defense (whichever has the highest bonus).

act_0616.subject: Action
act_0616.raw: Uses of Intimidation in ac- tion rounds are generally standard actions, although you can attempt to deceive as a move action by taking a –5 penalty to your check.

act_0617.subject: Character
act_0617.raw: At the GM’s discretion, characters may have to spend an additional Languages rank to be literate in a language with a different alphabet or style of writing from the character’s native language (such as Arabic, Japanese kanji or Russian Cyrillic for an English speaker).

act_0618.subject: Action
act_0618.raw: So a DC 25 action (a formidable task) requires a bonus of +15 in order to have a 55% chance of success (on a roll of 10 or higher).

act_0619.subject: Modifier
act_0619.raw: If you want to know what bonus is required to have a 55% chance of succeeding at a particular task, just sub- Circumstance modifiers (see their description in The Ba- sics chapter) are one of the GM’s best tools.

act_0620.subject: Attack
act_0620.raw: Whether or not the hero is successful in stopping the trap depends on the outcome of the attack against the bad guy.

act_0621.subject: Action
act_0621.raw: For detailed examples of how to use checks in the game and their effects, see the Action & Adventure chapter.

act_0622.subject: Check
act_0622.raw: Checks are made against a difficulty class or DC, a num- ber set by the GM, which your check must equal or exceed to achieve success.

act_0623.subject: Perception
act_0623.raw: DIFFICULTY (DC) EXAMPLE (SKILL USED) Very easy (0) Notice something in plain sight (Perception) Easy (5) Climb a knotted rope (Athletics) Average (10) Tough (15) Challenging (20) Formidable (25) Heroic (30) Super-heroic (35) Nigh-impossible (40) Hear an approaching security guard (Perception) Disarm an explosive (Technology) Swim against a strong current (Athletics) Climb a wet, slippery rock-face (Athletics) Overcome a sophisticated security system (Technology) Convinces the guards, even though you’re not wearing an ID badge and aren’t on their list, they should let you into the building (Deception) Track a trained commando through the jungle on a moonless night after 12 days of rainfall (Perception) TASK USING...

act_0624.subject: Character
act_0624.raw: If a character’s routine check result is not up to a task, the player still has the option to roll the die, since the task is by definition not routine for that character.

act_0625.subject: Action
act_0625.raw: (Choose One) 6, Perception 4, Technology 6 POWERS DODGE +4 PARRY +4 FORTITUDE +4 TOUGHNESS +0 SUPERHUMAN/VESSEL DODGE +4 PARRY +0 FORTITUDE +2 TOUGHNESS +0 WILL +6 WILL +6 If you have the Man of Action Abilities, don’t roll for your Offensive Power, instead, take Find Weakness:

act_0626.subject: Action
act_0626.raw: Power-lifting 2 • 8 points *The first number is if you have the Man of Action set of Abilities and the second number if you have the Superhu- man or Vessel set of Abilities.

act_0627.subject: Attack
act_0627.raw: For example, you can feint and have your target vulnerable against one or more allies next attack(s), rather than yours.

act_0628.subject: Action
act_0628.raw: The interaction skill check requires its normal action, and the affected allies must be capable of interacting with you (or at least seeing the set-up) to benefit from it.

act_0629.subject: Action
act_0629.raw: Gamemasters should generally allow you to control your sidekick, although sidekicks re- main NPCs and the GM has final say in their actions.

act_0630.subject: Check
act_0630.raw: When you support a team check (see Team Checks in The Basics chapter) you have a +5 circumstance bonus to your check.

act_0631.subject: Damage
act_0631.raw: COMBAT, RANKED You have a +1 damage bonus with thrown weapons per rank in this advantage.

act_0632.subject: Effect
act_0632.raw: TRACKING SKILL SKILL You can use the Perception skill to visually follow tracks like the Tracking Senses effect (see the Powers chapter).

act_0633.subject: Action
act_0633.raw: TAKEDOWN COMBAT, RANKED (2) If you render a minion incapacitated with an attack, you get an immediate extra attack as a free action against an- other minion within range and adjacent to the previous target’s location.

act_0634.subject: Check
act_0634.raw: While in the trance you add your Awareness rank to your Stamina rank to de- termine how long you can hold your breath and you use the higher of your Fortitude or Will defenses for resistance checks against suffocation (see Suffocation, page 238).

act_0635.subject: Check
act_0635.raw: It requires a Perception check with a DC equal to your Awareness check result to determine you’re not dead because your bodily functions are so slow.

act_0636.subject: Power
act_0636.raw: A Totem who was granted his powers by mystical or higher beings may be required to use his powers in their service.

act_0637.subject: Power
act_0637.raw: The Totem may have an unusual appear- ance or display strange powers that frighten the public.

act_0638.subject: Power
act_0638.raw: They are versatile and have many of the skills of Martial Artists and Weapon Mas- ters but the strength and power to fight alongside the Paragons and Powerhouses.

act_0639.subject: Character
act_0639.raw: They are made against an- other character’s check result as the DC.

act_0640.subject: Action
act_0640.raw: Active defenses in combat, where characters are focusing on other actions, are generally routine opposition, which is why attack checks are made against a DC of 10 + the ap- propriate defense.

act_0641.subject: Ability
act_0641.raw: In cases where a check is a simple test of one character’s capability against another, with no luck involved, both participants compare their appropriate ranks.

act_0642.subject: Character
act_0642.raw: Note this does not in- clude the use of extra effort (see page 19) to temporarily increase a character’s rank, which can affect the outcome of a comparison check, nor does it include things like ma- neuvers, tricks, or other ways of trying to affect the out- come.

act_0643.subject: Action
act_0643.raw: Take a standard action and make an interaction skill check against your target’s opposing check (Insight or Will defense).

act_0644.subject: Action
act_0644.raw: FAST GRAB COMBAT When you hit with an unarmed attack you can immedi- ately make a grab check against that opponent as a free action (see Grab, page 248).

act_0645.subject: Effect
act_0645.raw: SKILL You have a particular type of opponent you’ve studied or are especially effective against.

act_0646.subject: Check
act_0646.raw: GENERAL You have a +5 bonus on checks to avoid becoming fa- tigued and checks to hold your breath, avoid damage from starvation or thirst, avoid damage from hot or cold environments, and to resist suffocation and drowning.

act_0647.subject: Action
act_0647.raw: COMBAT You have no penalty to your attack check when attempt- ing to disarm an opponent and they do not get the op- portunity to disarm you (see Disarm in the Action & Ad- venture chapter).

act_0648.subject: Effect
act_0648.raw: POWERS level mon effect used to create it, is subject to the normal limits, and power cannot have minions of its own, either from this effect or the Minions advantage.

act_0649.subject: Action
act_0649.raw: Minions have their own initiative (see Initiative in the Action & Adventure chapter) and act starting on the round after you summon them.

act_0650.subject: Modifier
act_0650.raw: Do not apply the Active modifier to Heroic minions, as this modifier already includes it.

act_0651.subject: Check
act_0651.raw: Targets failing the Fortitude resistance check against your effect DC become fatigued, then exhausted, and finally asleep as they succumb.

act_0652.subject: Action
act_0652.raw: If you have Multiple Minions (see following) you may take a standard action to summon any number of minions up to your maximum amount.

act_0653.subject: Ability
act_0653.raw: The subject granted the use of the effect con- trols its configuration, if appropriate for its descrip- tors (although you retain the ability to withdraw use of the effect altogether whenever you wish).

act_0654.subject: Effect
act_0654.raw: A Variable effect with Affects Others may have the Ranged extra to improve the range at which you can grant the effect to another.

act_0655.subject: Check
act_0655.raw: A successful resistance check reduces the Area effect to half its normal rank against that target (round down, minimum of 1 rank).

act_0656.subject: Effect
act_0656.raw: An effect must be at least close range in order to apply Area (personal range effects work only on the user by definition).

act_0657.subject: Roll
act_0657.raw: Agility • Trained Only Use Acrobatics to flip, dive, roll, tumble, and perform other acrobatic maneuvers, as well as keeping your balance un- der difficult circumstances.

act_0658.subject: Check
act_0658.raw: BALANCING You can keep your balance and move along a precari- ous surface at your ground speed minus 1 rank with a successful Acrobatics check against the surface’s DC.

act_0659.subject: Check
act_0659.raw: If you fail a resistance check while balancing, make an- other immediate Acrobatics check against the original DC to avoid falling.

act_0660.subject: Character
act_0660.raw: No matter which option you use when creating a character, feel free to alter the results of your rolls to make sure you get a character you find interesting.

act_0661.subject: Character
act_0661.raw: Just like the characters in the comics, your hero needs at least two Complications;

act_0662.subject: Character
act_0662.raw: Once you have your character’s various traits and Compli- cations determined and on paper, you’re ready to play!

act_0663.subject: Defense
act_0663.raw: Usually, this process is as simple as replacing ranks of one effect for equivalent ranks of another ef- fect, but remember, if you choose to delete a rank or two from Presence and use those points to buy some ranks of Stealth or to increase your Dodge defense, for example, you’ll need to make sure you adjust any skill totals that changed because of the hero’s lowered Presence rank.

act_0664.subject: Roll
act_0664.raw: Bandit has Sleight of Hand +12 and adds that to the roll of a die.

act_0665.subject: Perception
act_0665.raw: The guard, with a Perception skill of only +5, doesn’t have a prayer of noticing his gun being eased out of its holster, but the GM rolls anyway and gets a total of 20.

act_0666.subject: Check
act_0666.raw: FLAT • -1 POINT PER RANK If Activation is not automatic, apply the Check Required flaw to the entire power as well and have the player make the necessary check in order to activate the power.

act_0667.subject: Action
act_0667.raw: If the check fails, the power does not activate, and the character has to take the activation action to try again.

act_0668.subject: Effect
act_0668.raw: The GM should decide if allow- ing a permanent effect to have an Activation is appropri- ate based on the specific effect and any others it is com- bined with in the power.

act_0669.subject: Power
act_0669.raw: Stonewall has the power to turn into a super-strong rock-form.

act_0670.subject: Action
act_0670.raw: Stonewall’s player applies the Activation flaw to the power, saying Stonewall has to concentrate and take a standard action to assume his rock-form.

act_0671.subject: Action
act_0671.raw: That reduces the total cost of all three effects by 2 power points and means unless Stonewall takes a standard action to activate his rock-form, he cannot use any of the power’s effects, even including permanent ones like Protection.

act_0672.subject: Check
act_0672.raw: An effect with this flaw requires a check of some sort—usually a skill check—with a base difficulty of 10, +1 for each addition- al rank in Check Required.

act_0673.subject: Action
act_0673.raw: If the check fails, the effect doesn’t work, although the action required to use it is expended (so attempting to activate a standard action effect takes a stan- dard action whether the check is successful or not).

act_0674.subject: Character
act_0674.raw: If the check succeeds, the character gains the use of 1 ef- fect rank per point the check exceeds the DC.

act_0675.subject: Character
act_0675.raw: Thus a check result of 14 allows the character to use up to 4 ranks of the effect.

act_0676.subject: Attack
act_0676.raw: For a broader bonus to attack checks that is less than simply raising Fighting rank, see the Close Attack advantage in the Advantages chapter.

act_0677.subject: Action
act_0677.raw: Uses of Deception in action rounds are generally standard actions, although you can attempt to deceive as a move action by taking a –5 penalty to your check.

act_0678.subject: Check
act_0678.raw: If the target’s Insight check succeeds by 11 or more, he has seen through the deception, and would have refused even if it had not placed unusual demands on him (that is, even without the +10 modifier).

act_0679.subject: Character
act_0679.raw: TALENT In game terms there’s no difference between a character who has ranks in a skill because of extensive training and an- other whose skill ranks represent a natural talent or aptitude for the skill.

act_0680.subject: Character
act_0680.raw: When allocating skill ranks for your character consider not just the character’s role as a hero but also the various other skills the character may have picked up in day-to-day life.

act_0681.subject: Character
act_0681.raw: Characters working with technology may have the Technology skill even if it doesn’t apply to their powers.

act_0682.subject: Expertise
act_0682.raw: A pilot should have Vehicles, while a doctor should have Treatment in addition to Expertise:

act_0683.subject: Attack
act_0683.raw: For more signifi- cant obscuring of senses (via darkness, fog, etc.) use an Area Concealment Attack effect (see Concealment in this chapter).

act_0684.subject: Effect
act_0684.raw: There- fore, any harm directed specifically against your Extra Limb(s) has no effect.

act_0685.subject: Ability
act_0685.raw: 1 point per rank You have one or more minor features, effects granting you an occasionally useful ability, one per rank.

act_0686.subject: Character
act_0686.raw: There’s no need to define every possible Fea- ture a character may have down to the last detail.

act_0687.subject: Effect
act_0687.raw: POWERS As with the Feature effect, a Feature extra should be sig- nificant enough to be worth at least 1 power point and not solely based on the power’s descriptors.

act_0688.subject: Attack
act_0688.raw: The Homing effect uses the same accurate sense as the original attack to “track” its target, so concealment effec- tive against that sense may confuse the effect and cause it to miss.

act_0689.subject: Attack
act_0689.raw: If a Homing attack misses due to concealment, it has lost its “lock” on the target and does not get any further chances to hit.

act_0690.subject: Effect
act_0690.raw: Any effect with a resistance difficulty modifier equal to or less than half the Impervious rank (rounded up) has no effect.

act_0691.subject: Attack
act_0691.raw: If the effect requires an initial attack check, no ad- ditional attack check is needed to maintain it on a tar- get, but subsequent rounds of effect also do not ben- efit from critical hits.

act_0692.subject: Attack
act_0692.raw: Increasing the range of an effect from personal to close requires either the Affects Others or Attack extras (see their descriptions).

act_0693.subject: Effect
act_0693.raw: Making a close ef- fect into a perception ranged effect requires two applica- tions of this extra, for +2 cost per rank.

act_0694.subject: Character
act_0694.raw: POWERS In many instances, players come up with creative uses for their characters’ descriptors.

act_0695.subject: Character
act_0695.raw: So if a fire-using character wants to use a tiny amount of his flame blast power to light some candles, or the electrical-controlling character wants to use some of his power output to act as a living battery to jump-start a car, go for it.

act_0696.subject: Effect
act_0696.raw: Creative uses of descriptors with no real game effect are freebies:

act_0697.subject: Effect
act_0697.raw: Situations where creative uses of descriptors have a significant game effect can be handled as power stunts:

act_0698.subject: Effect
act_0698.raw: pick the effect that best suits the desired outcome and treat it as an Alternate Effect of the power the hero wants to use, with descrip- tors assigned as appropriate.

act_0699.subject: Attack
act_0699.raw: If an electrical-controlling hero wants to use his power like a living defibrillator to save a heart-attack victim, for example, that can be a Healing power stunt.

act_0700.subject: Power
act_0700.raw: The GM agrees and so the heroine’s powers have no origin or source descriptors.

act_0701.subject: Power
act_0701.raw: Temporarily changing a descriptor can be a use of extra ef- fort, like any other power stunt.

act_0702.subject: Damage
act_0702.raw: For example, a hero might change the result of an electrical Damage effect to a mag- netic Move Object effect for one use.

act_0703.subject: Effect
act_0703.raw: Heroes able to ignore the effects of deep space must have Immunity to suffocation, vacuum, and radiation, at a mini- mum.

act_0704.subject: Attack
act_0704.raw: If your result equals or exceeds the target’s defense, your attack hits and may have some effect.

act_0705.subject: Attack
act_0705.raw: To find out if it’s a critical hit, determine if the attack check total is equal to or greater than the target’s defense.

act_0706.subject: Character
act_0706.raw: Characters with the Improved Critical advantage (see page 136) can score a threat on a natural result less than 20, although they still automatically hit only on a natural 20.

act_0707.subject: Check
act_0707.raw: Against a min- ion, this bypasses the resistance check entirely;

act_0708.subject: Attack
act_0708.raw: The target makes resis- tance checks against the attack’s initial and added effects separately.

act_0709.subject: Attack
act_0709.raw: The critical hit results in an alter- nate effect for the attack, like a use of extra effort for a power stunt (see Extra Effort in The Basics chap- ter), except the character suffers no fatigue as a re- sult.

act_0710.subject: Power
act_0710.raw: Some Psychics use their powers for good, because they feel they must have been given their powers to help others.

act_0711.subject: Power
act_0711.raw: Because mental powers often require some amount of focus;

act_0712.subject: Character
act_0712.raw: Shapeshifters include characters who actually change their shape to become animals, machines, mythic creatures, or hu- manoid monsters, as well as characters who can change their density, grow, shrink (or both!), or stretch to fantastic lengths.

act_0713.subject: Action
act_0713.raw: Immobile characters have no movement speed and cannot move from the spot they occupy, although they are still capable of taking actions un- less prohibited by another condition.

act_0714.subject: Character
act_0714.raw: Transformed characters have some or all of their traits altered by an outside agency.

act_0715.subject: Character
act_0715.raw: Subjects have full concealment from all of a character’s unaware senses.

act_0716.subject: Character
act_0716.raw: The character has temporarily lost power points in a trait.

act_0717.subject: Check
act_0717.raw: Targets fail- ing the Fortitude resistance check against your effect DC become dazed, stunned, and finally incapacitated, passing out from the lack of oxygen.

act_0718.subject: Effect
act_0718.raw: A failed attempt to resist the ongoing effect of Suffocation causes the target’s condition to worsen by one degree.

act_0719.subject: Action
act_0719.raw: (mass rank 0) of additional mass a distance rank equal to your effect rank as a move action.

act_0720.subject: Effect
act_0720.raw: Each rank of Super-Speed gives you the effects of Improved Initiative as an Enhanced Trait, Quickness, and Speed, with a ground speed rank equal to your power rank.

act_0721.subject: Action
act_0721.raw: So with Super-Speed 10, for example, you have +40 to initiative checks, can perform routine actions normally requiring two hours in just 6 seconds, and have a ground speed of 2,000 miles per hour!

act_0722.subject: Ability
act_0722.raw: Heroes with Super-Speed often have additional powers based on their speed, particularly things like Air Control (whipping up powerful winds, see Element Control, previously) or modifiers to their Strength Damage like Area or Multiattack to represent the ability to make a rapid series of attacks in a single turn.

act_0723.subject: Defense
act_0723.raw: Abilities 28 + Powers 0 + Advantages 3 + Skills 22 + Defenses 8 = 61 Government agents include members of organizations like the FBI, CIA, DEA, ATF, and even the DEO.

act_0724.subject: Damage
act_0724.raw: Fire Elementals, in particular, are apt to inadvertently cause destruction in their wake, but even Water Elementals may cause property damage by just their presence.

act_0725.subject: Effect
act_0725.raw: In addition to being able to fire powerful energy blasts, Energy Control- lers often utilize their powers to produce a wide variety of unusual and useful effects.

act_0726.subject: Character
act_0726.raw: Although descriptors don’t always have significant game effects in MUTANTS & MASTERMINDS, they’re perhaps the most important element in providing color and character to the powers of heroes and villains.

act_0727.subject: Power
act_0727.raw: Since they involve a change in the sub- ject’s DNA, mutant powers—or at least the potential for them—are inheritable.

act_0728.subject: Ability
act_0728.raw: SOURCE A power’s source differs from its origin in that the origin is where the potential or ability to use the power comes from (where the character got the power in the first place), while source is where the power’s effect comes from, or where the power draws its energy.

act_0729.subject: Check
act_0729.raw: Anyone you touch or that touches you must make a Toughness resistance check against your aura’s Damage rank.

act_0730.subject: Effect
act_0730.raw: As with all flaws, the effect must still cost at least 1 power point.

act_0731.subject: Effect
act_0731.raw: If you have one you can acquire others as Alternate Effects, but you can then only use and maintain one at a time.

act_0732.subject: Effect
act_0732.raw: To use or maintain multiple Environment effects simultane- ously, add their costs together for the effect’s total cost per rank or apply the Selective modifier, allowing you to mix-and-match effects.

act_0733.subject: Action
act_0733.raw: Enhanced Trait, Fades, Reaction • as base trait You take the energy from a particular type of attack, chosen when you take this power, and use it to en- hance one of your traits.

act_0734.subject: Attack
act_0734.raw: The en- hancement to your trait is equal to your power rank or the attack’s rank, whichever is less.

act_0735.subject: Attack
act_0735.raw: Rebound has rank 11 Energy Absorption that applies the energy of kinetic attacks to Strength.

act_0736.subject: Attack
act_0736.raw: Absorption often includes the additional ef- fect of Protection (Impervious and often Limited to the absorbed type of attack) or even Immunity to the ab- sorbed attack.

act_0737.subject: Action
act_0737.raw: Since interaction skills are intended for dealing with others so- cially, they have certain requirements.

act_0738.subject: Check
act_0738.raw: If they can’t hear or understand you for some reason, you have a –5 circumstance penalty to your skill check (see Circumstance Modifiers in The Basics).

act_0739.subject: Action
act_0739.raw: The GM decides if a particular use of an interaction skill is effective against a group, and may apply modifiers depending on the situation.

act_0740.subject: Ability
act_0740.raw: Characters lacking the ability to use manipulation skills can still have ranks in them and use them to oversee or assist the work of others (see Team Checks, page 16).

act_0741.subject: Character
act_0741.raw: Characters may be able to use skills for tasks other than those given here.

act_0742.subject: Ability
act_0742.raw: Ability • Trained Only • Interaction • Manipulation • Requires Tools The skill name line and the line below it contain the fol- lowing information:

act_0743.subject: Character
act_0743.raw: If “Trained Only” is absent, untrained characters (those with 0 ranks in the skill) may use it.

act_0744.subject: Damage
act_0744.raw: GADGETS & GEAR 213 Replacing damaged or destroyed equipment requires only time and resources, although the GM has the final say as to how much time.

act_0745.subject: Power
act_0745.raw: While equipment is useful it does have its limits, particu- larly when compared to powers or devices.

act_0746.subject: Damage
act_0746.raw: Equipment is less expensive—it’s cheaper to have a handgun than a Damage power or even a super-science blaster weapon— but equipment is also more limited.

act_0747.subject: Character
act_0747.raw: Devices are not so limited and characters are assumed to have an easy means of car- rying and transporting them.

act_0748.subject: Effect
act_0748.raw: Equipment bonuses are limited compared to the bonuses granted by other effects.

act_0749.subject: Character
act_0749.raw: Equipment may be lost or taken away from the character with im- punity, and the GM may have equipment fail, run out of ammo or fuel, or otherwise malfunction as a complica- tion.

act_0750.subject: Check
act_0750.raw: Heroes often have access to special government channels, while a successful Technology skill check (DC 25) can grant a user illegal access to re- stricted systems.

act_0751.subject: Character
act_0751.raw: This allows characters to make full use of the Technology skill and the computer can be pro- grammed to handle routine base functions (including monitoring communications channels and controlling defensive systems).

act_0752.subject: Check
act_0752.raw: Skill checks to locate the headquarters have their DC increased by +10.

act_0753.subject: Attack
act_0753.raw: A defense system can have any attack effect with a cost no greater than twice the HQ power level.

act_0754.subject: Attack
act_0754.raw: Their attack bonus is equal to the power level.

act_0755.subject: Character
act_0755.raw: COMPLICATIONS AND UP-FRONT REWARDS Some roleplaying game systems include complications, disadvantages, or similar problematic character traits which offer “bonus points” for creating the character;

act_0756.subject: Character
act_0756.raw: Plus, after they have “paid out” their initial benefit, front-loaded negative traits are nothing but a burden to the character from that point forward, leading players to try and avoid or mitigate them as much as possible.

act_0757.subject: Damage
act_0757.raw: Afflic- tion (see the Powers chapter) is the typical effect, but some weaknesses inflict outright Damage, Weaken the target, or have some other effect.

act_0758.subject: Effect
act_0758.raw: The Communication effect still has its proximate range, and the GM may rule certain subjects “out of range” of the effect, depending on their relative positions in the other dimension.

act_0759.subject: Toughness
act_0759.raw: Steel cuffs have Toughness 9 and are DC 20 to escape using Sleight of Hand (or Technology to pick the lock).

act_0760.subject: Check
act_0760.raw: It requires an Investigation or Perception check against the results of the Sleight of Hand check used to conceal the microphone to find it.

act_0761.subject: Action
act_0761.raw: It requires a successful attack check to plant a tracer on a target (or a Sleight of Hand check to plant it outside of action time).

act_0762.subject: Attack
act_0762.raw: These tinted eye-coverings provide pro- tection against blinding light, granting a +5 equipment bonus to resistance checks against visual Dazzle attacks involving bright light.

act_0763.subject: Power
act_0763.raw: “Good” and “evil” are common abstract moral sourc- es of powers, but others may include chaos, law, an- archy, order, justice, balance, neutrality, reason, and so forth.

act_0764.subject: Power
act_0764.raw: Although tech- nological power sources often involve Devices or Equipment, they don’t necessary have to;

act_0765.subject: Effect
act_0765.raw: MEDIUM A power’s medium is what the power uses to accomplish its effect(s).

act_0766.subject: Effect
act_0766.raw: a psionic power uses psionic energy to power and accomplish its effects, likewise, a divine power often uses divine energy to power and accomplish its effects.

act_0767.subject: Damage
act_0767.raw: For example, the power to throw fireballs granted by the God of Fire is a bestowed origin with a divine source, using fire as the me- dium to cause its Damage effect.

act_0768.subject: Character
act_0768.raw: The change is necessary because the Summon power your character has influ- ences the number of points available to spend on other sections of the character.

act_0769.subject: Check
act_0769.raw: If the Heal- ing check fails, you must wait the normal recovery time or use extra effort to try again.

act_0770.subject: Attack
act_0770.raw: Applied to Ranged Healing (following), Per- ception Ranged Healing does not require an attack check to “touch” the subject.

act_0771.subject: Attack
act_0771.raw: Ranged Healing requires an attack check to “touch” the subject with the Healing effect.

act_0772.subject: Damage
act_0772.raw: One Type of Damage (such as energy or blud- geoning damage), Objects (in conjunction with Affects Objects), Others (you can’t use Healing on yourself), or Self (you can only use Healing on yourself).

act_0773.subject: Check
act_0773.raw: If the sub- ject has been dead for fewer minutes than your Healing rank, makes a DC 20 Healing check.

act_0774.subject: Effect
act_0774.raw: When you successfully cure someone else of a condition, you acquire the condition yourself and must Illusions have no substance and cannot have any real- world effect.

act_0775.subject: Check
act_0775.raw: A successful Insight check against an illusion (DC 10 + Illusion rank) reveals it as false.

act_0776.subject: Attack
act_0776.raw: The GM rolls attack checks against the heroes.

act_0777.subject: Toughness
act_0777.raw: Two smugglers shoot at Ultramarine and three shoot at Princess, but they both have Impervious Toughness 8.

act_0778.subject: Attack
act_0778.raw: Compares Rook’s attack against DC 10, a definite hit and an automatic critical for +5 damage.

act_0779.subject: Check
act_0779.raw: The GM checks the net’s Toughness of 9 against DC 25 (15 + 5 for the talons + 5 for the critical), generating a 23 result.

act_0780.subject: Roll
act_0780.raw: Trawler has Toughness 10 and the GM rolls a 9.

act_0781.subject: Dodge
act_0781.raw: A creature with no Agility is unable to move its body under its own power and has no Dodge de- fense.

act_0782.subject: Ability
act_0782.raw: MUTANTS & MASTERMINDS heroes cannot be absent an ability without Gamemaster permission, as it can have significant effects on the character and the game.

act_0783.subject: Attack
act_0783.raw: A creature with no Fighting is incapable of making any sort of close attack (but may still be able to launch ranged attacks, if it has Dexterity).

act_0784.subject: Action
act_0784.raw: Anything with no Intellect is immune to mental effects and in- teraction skills and has no Will defense.

act_0785.subject: Action
act_0785.raw: Objects are immune to mental effects and interaction skills, and have no Inanimate objects have no abilities other than their Toughness.

act_0786.subject: Action
act_0786.raw: Animate, but nonliving, constructs such as ro- bots or zombies have Strength, Agility, and Dexterity, and may have ranks of Awareness and Presence (if aware of their environment or capable of interaction), and Fighting (if able to make close attacks).

act_0787.subject: Attack
act_0787.raw: A hero’s defenses are abilities used to avoid such things, determining the difficulty to affect a hero with an attack, or to make resistance checks against them.

act_0788.subject: Action
act_0788.raw: Dodge and Parry defenses require a measure of action to be fully effective.

act_0789.subject: Defense
act_0789.raw: One use of defenses is determining a defense class, or the difficulty class to affect a target with a particular at- tack.

act_0790.subject: Attack
act_0790.raw: So hitting a character with a ranged attack goes against Dodge defense, giving the attack a DC of (Dodge + 10).

act_0791.subject: Defense
act_0791.raw: Similarly, affecting someone with a mental power goes against Will defense, with a DC of (Will + 10), and so forth.

act_0792.subject: Ability
act_0792.raw: Defenses are also used to measure the ability to over- come certain effects, involving a resistance check of the defense plus a die roll against a difficulty class determined by the effect or hazard.

act_0793.subject: Character
act_0793.raw: When things start happening quickly, MUTANTS & MASTER- characters use their initiative bonuses to determine who goes first.

act_0794.subject: Character
act_0794.raw: Stronger characters can lift greater weights, more agile characters have better balance, tougher characters can soak up more damage, and so forth.

act_0795.subject: Defense
act_0795.raw: Sometimes your rank is used to calculate another value, such as when you use your Agility to determine how good you are at avoiding harm with your reflexes (your Dodge defense).

act_0796.subject: Ability
act_0796.raw: peak of human achievement Low superhuman Moderate superhuman High superhuman Very high superhuman Cosmic Although a rank of 7 is defined as “the peak of human achievement” in an ability on the Ability Benchmarks table, a character with an ability rank greater than 7 isn’t necessarily “non-human,” merely superhuman in comparison to ordinary people.

act_0797.subject: Ability
act_0797.raw: A character can have a superhuman ability rank without necessarily being anything other than an amazingly talented, well-trained human being.

act_0798.subject: Dodge
act_0798.raw: So at Growth 8, you have +8 Strength and Stamina, +4 to Intimidation, +1 Speed, but -8 to Stealth, –4 Dodge and Parry, and you are size rank 0 (around 30 feet tall).

act_0799.subject: Check
act_0799.raw: If the Healing check fails, you must wait one minute or use extra effort in order to try again.

act_0800.subject: Check

rel_0102.raw: Characters with the Inventor and Artificer advantages can create temporary devices for use in an adventure.

rel_0103.raw: Gamemasters may require characters to spend a hero point to make temporary use of a device that doesn’t be- long to them, similar to performing a power stunt without suffering fatigue.

rel_0104.raw: A battlesuit with an array of weapons may have a primary attack effect and several others as Alternate Effects (see the Alternate Effect modifier in the Powers chapter).

rel_0105.raw: Costumes may have other properties and can even be the source of a hero’s powers, such as in the case of battlesuits (previ- ously).

rel_0106.raw: POWERS 143 In some settings, the Gamemaster may require certain descriptors for all powers.

rel_0107.raw: For ex- ample, if all characters with powers are mutants, then all powers have the “mutant” descriptor by definition, un- less the player comes up with a good explanation why they should not.

rel_0108.raw: If all superhumans are psychic mutants, then all powers have both the “psychic” and “mutant” descriptors.

rel_0109.raw: A character who breaks this guideline—say the one alien in a setting where all powers are otherwise mutant in origin—might have a Benefit (unusual origin) or face certain complica- tions, possibly both.

rel_0110.raw: Attack effects require a standard action to use.

rel_0111.raw: Control effects require a standard action to initiate, but can then usually be sustained.

rel_0112.raw: Control effects used against unwilling targets usually require an at- tack check and allow a resistance check, the same for the hazards they are capable of causing, such as creating intense cold or dropping a heavy object on someone.

rel_0118.raw: Vehicles can even have power effects of their own.

rel_0127.raw: Arrays should have some unifying theme beyond “all the powers I want my hero to have,” and Gamemasters should feel free to veto inap- propriate arrays lacking a strong theme.

rel_0466.raw: This type of Illusion has the Resistible by Will flaw and the Selective extra, since the illusionist can choose whether or not to project the illusion into a par- ticular subject’s mind, and therefore decides who can or cannot perceive the illusion.

rel_0493.raw: It proved very popular and when we had the chance, we offered up the idea of including an updated version of it in the Hero’s Handbook to the fans and their response was a resound- ing, “Yes!” This updated version of the Quickstart Character Genera- tor includes clearer instructions, some new powers and options for some of the archetypes, and fixes to a couple of the archetypes to make sure they didn’t break any of the rules.

rel_0497.raw: Sometimes that requires a number of rolls, sometimes just a few.

rel_0498.raw: For ex- ample, the Battlesuit, Mystic, and Weather Controller all require a fair number of rolls to determine their Powers and Alternate Effects, while the Mimic, Shapeshifter, and Summoner require fewer rolls, because they have only a few powers.

rel_0499.raw: All the Archetypes’ effects include names for their powers, such as the Battlesuit’s “Gravity Drivers,” which allow it to fly, but you’re welcome (encouraged, even!) to change the power’s name to something more to your liking.

rel_0561.raw: FLAT • -1 POINT PER RANK If Activation is not automatic, apply the Check Required flaw to the entire power as well and have the player make the necessary check in order to activate the power.

rel_0562.raw: If the check fails, the power does not activate, and the character has to take the activation action to try again.

rel_0563.raw: The GM should decide if allow- ing a permanent effect to have an Activation is appropri- ate based on the specific effect and any others it is com- bined with in the power.

rel_0564.raw: Stonewall has the power to turn into a super-strong rock-form.

rel_0565.raw: Stonewall’s player applies the Activation flaw to the power, saying Stonewall has to concentrate and take a standard action to assume his rock-form.

rel_0566.raw: That reduces the total cost of all three effects by 2 power points and means unless Stonewall takes a standard action to activate his rock-form, he cannot use any of the power’s effects, even including permanent ones like Protection.

rel_0567.raw: An effect with this flaw requires a check of some sort—usually a skill check—with a base difficulty of 10, +1 for each addition- al rank in Check Required.

rel_0568.raw: If the check fails, the effect doesn’t work, although the action required to use it is expended (so attempting to activate a standard action effect takes a stan- dard action whether the check is successful or not).

rel_0569.raw: If the check succeeds, the character gains the use of 1 ef- fect rank per point the check exceeds the DC.

rel_0570.raw: Thus a check result of 14 allows the character to use up to 4 ranks of the effect.

rel_0571.raw: Uses of Deception in action rounds are generally standard actions, although you can attempt to deceive as a move action by taking a –5 penalty to your check.

rel_0572.raw: If the target’s Insight check succeeds by 11 or more, he has seen through the deception, and would have refused even if it had not placed unusual demands on him (that is, even without the +10 modifier).

rel_0573.raw: TALENT In game terms there’s no difference between a character who has ranks in a skill because of extensive training and an- other whose skill ranks represent a natural talent or aptitude for the skill.

rel_0574.raw: When allocating skill ranks for your character consider not just the character’s role as a hero but also the various other skills the character may have picked up in day-to-day life.
